Located in the area formerly known as {{ill|Mimasaka Province|ja|美作国|12=draft}}, it is one of the "Mimasaka Three Hot Springs," together with [[:en:Yunogo_Onsen|Yunogo Onsen]] and {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|ceb|Yubara hotspring|12=draft}}. == Water Quality == * {{ill|Alkaline Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=draft}} – Known as the "Hot Spring for Beauties"主婦の友社（編）『主婦の友ベストBooks 見直したい日本の「美」 日本の名湯100選』 p.134 主婦の友社 2010th year 3月10日発行 ISBN 978-4-07-270188-1. ** Source temperature: 35 - 44 ℃ ** Discharge: 1000 (L/min) == Onsen Town == [[File:Okutsu Onsen 02.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Footbath area]] A hot spring town is formed around the Okutsu Bridge spanning the {{ill|Yoshii River|ja|吉井川|12=draft}}, with day-trip bathing facilities available. Along the riverbank, there are two {{ill|Outdoor Baths|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo|1=Rotenburo|ko|노천탕|1=Rotenburo|12=draft}}; one of these is known as "Sentaku-yu" (Laundry Bath), where the practice of '''foot‑stepping laundry''' is famous. This method utilizes the drifting effect of the hot spring water—with a pH of 9.2—to aid in washing, a technique developed while keeping watch for bears and wolves in the surrounding mountains【湯の心旅】Okutsu Onsen (Okayama Prefecture) heralds spring with "foot‑stepping laundry" – washing away fatigue from within and without, as reported in the {{ill|Nikkei|ja|日本経済新聞|12=draft}} morning edition, December 21, 2020 (separate printing, NIKKEI Plus 1, page 13)主婦の友社（編）『主婦の友ベストBooks 見直したい日本の「美」 日本の名湯100選』 p.135 主婦の友社 2010th year 3月10日発行 ISBN 978-4-07-270188-1. Today, some locals still use the baths for laundry, and live demonstrations for tourists are held on Sundays and public holidays from March to November. Along National Route 179, there is a roadside station, {{ill|Michinoeki Okutsu Onsen|ja|道の駅奥津温泉|12=draft}}, though it does not include bathing facilities. Downstream, approximately 2 km from the laundry area along the Yoshii River, there are also {{ill|Han'nya-ji Onsen|ja|般若寺温泉|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ōdzuri Onsen|ja|大釣温泉|12=draft}}; these are believed to be sourced from the same fault line as the upstream Okutsu Onsen, and in some contexts the entire area is collectively referred to as "Okutsu Onsen"日本温泉科学会（監修）『図説 日本の温泉 ―170温泉のサイエンス―』 p.133 朝倉書店 2020th year 3月1日発行 ISBN 978-4-254-16075-8. == History == According to the {{ill|Onsen Origin Myth|lt=Hot Spring Founding Legend|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|12=draft}}, the hot spring was discovered by {{ill|Sukunabikona-no-mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=draft}}. During the {{ill|Edo Period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}, it served as a site for hot spring treatment ({{ill|Tōji|ja|湯治|1=Thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|1=Thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|12=draft}}) for the {{ill|Tsuyama Domain|ja|津山藩|12=draft}}, and the area around the present-day foot‑stepping laundry is said to have once housed a "Locked Bath" ({{ill|Kagiyu|ja|鍵湯|12=draft}}), from which usage was restricted by domain lord {{ill|Mori Tadamasu|ja|森忠政|12=draft}}, who stationed guards to lock it. The cosmetics company {{ill|KOSÉ|ja|コーセー|12=draft}} has developed a lotion based on the components of this hot spring. Foot‑stepping laundry has long been practiced; its process was even commemorated in a tanka by {{ill|Yosano Tekkan|ja|与謝野鉄幹|12=draft}}:
衣洗う　奥津のおとめ　河床の　清きに立ちて　踊るごと践む
However, a large fire in 1926 destroyed the hot spring town山村 順次 『47都道府県・温泉百科』 p.213 丸善出版 2015th year 12月30日発行 ISBN 978-4-621-08996-5. The area is also known as the setting of {{ill|Akitsu Onsen|ja|秋津温泉|12=draft}}, a novel by {{ill|Fujiwara Shini|ja|藤原審爾|12=draft}}, which was later adapted into a film in 1962 that boosted the prominence of Okutsu Onsen. On August 1, 1966, Okutsu Onsen was designated a {{ill|National Recreational Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|12=draft}}, and in 1991 it was designated as a {{ill|National Health Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保健温泉地|12=draft}}. An express bus service, nicknamed the "Romance Car," operated by {{ill|Chūtetsu Bus|ja|中鉄バス|12=draft}} connects {{ill|Okayama Station|ja|岡山駅|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tsuyama Station|ja|津山駅|12=draft}}, and Okutsu Onsen (stopping in front of the roadside station on National Route 179) with one round trip per day. == Access == * '''Automobile''': Approximately 25 minutes by car from the {{ill|Chūgoku Expressway|ja|中国自動車道|12=draft}} and {{ill|Inshō Interchange|ja|院庄インターチェンジ|12=draft}}. * '''Railway''': Alight at {{ill|Tsuyama Station|ja|津山駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|12=draft}} ({{ill|Himeshin Line|ja|姫新線|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tsuyama Line|ja|津山線|12=draft}}), then take the bus from bus terminal stop #5 operated by {{ill|Chūtetsu Hokubu Bus|ja|中鉄北部バス|12=draft}}: ** Board the Okutsu Onsen/Ishikoshi Line for about 50 minutes (operating 4 round trips per day; 2 round trips on weekends and holidays). ** Transfer to the {{ill|Gongo Bus|ja|ごんごバス|12=draft}} West Loop line, and at either "Marunaka Inshō Store" or "Plant 5," transfer to a community bus bound for the Kamisaihara Promotion Center (operating 4 round trips per day). * '''Highway Bus''': From {{ill|Namba|ja|なんば|12=draft}} (via the {{ill|Osaka City Air Terminal|ja|なんば|12=draft}}) and from {{ill|Shin-Osaka Station|ja|新大阪駅|12=draft}} (via the Hankyu High-Speed Bus Shin-Osaka Terminal at the North Exit), take the {{ill|Nikkō Kōsokubasu|ja|日交高速バス|12=draft}} on the line from {{ill|Kurayoshi City|ja|倉吉市|12=draft}} to {{ill|Kobe City|ja|神戸市|12=draft}}/Osaka. Alight at Okutsu Onsen-guchi (in front of the roadside station on {{ill|National Route 179|ja|国道179号|12=draft}}); there is one round trip per day. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{WikiPortal|Hot Springs}} * [[:en:List_of_hot_springs_in_Japan|List of Hot Spring Resorts in Japan]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Okutsu Onsen}} * [https://www.mto.ne.jp/okutsuonsenkk/ Okutsu Onsen Tourism Association] * [http://kanko.town.kagamino.lg.jp/hot_spring/okutsu-spa/ Kagamino Town Tourism Navi: Okutsu Onsen] * [https://afw-at.com/page.php?id=146 Okutsu Onsen] – At Town Web Magazine ''' ''' '''''' {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|奥津温泉|version=104384327|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Okutsu Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot Springs in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreational Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Geography of Kagamino Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Legends]] [[Category:Onsen Origin Myth]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Un11]] }} [[sn:Okutsu Onsen]] [[ja:奥津温泉]] [[zh:温泉]] [[ja:嬉野温泉]] [[ja:武雄温泉]] [[ja:日本書紀]] [[ja:南紀白浜温泉]] [[ja:行基]] [[ja:空海]] [[ja:一遍]] [[ja:仏教]] [[ja:坂上田村麻呂]] [[ja:高野聖]] [[ja:大友頼泰]] [[ja:浜脇温泉]] [[ja:別府温泉]] [[ja:鉄輪温泉]] [[ja:火男火売神社]] [[ja:下呂温泉]] [[ja:山中温泉]] [[ja:道後温泉]] [[ja:城崎温泉]] [[ja:湯田温泉]] [[ja:三朝温泉]] [[ja:山鹿温泉]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] [[ja:老神温泉]] [[zh:温泉神社]] [[zh:温泉寺]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] [[en:Okutsu Onsen]]

Located in the area formerly known as {{ill|Mimasaka Province|ja|美作国|qq=draft}}, it is one of the "Mimasaka Three Hot Springs," together with [[:en:Yunogo_Onsen|Yunogo Onsen]] and {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|ceb|Yubara hotspring|qq=draft}}. == Water Quality == * {{ill|Alkaline Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|qq=draft}} – Known as the "Hot Spring for Beauties"主婦の友社（編）『主婦の友ベストBooks 見直したい日本の「美」 日本の名湯100選』 p.134 主婦の友社 2010th year 3月10日発行 ISBN 978-4-07-270188-1. ** Source temperature: 35 - 44 ℃ ** Discharge: 1000 (L/min) == Onsen Town == [[File:Okutsu Onsen 02.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Footbath area]] A hot spring town is formed around the Okutsu Bridge spanning the {{ill|Yoshii River|ja|吉井川|qq=draft}}, with day-trip bathing facilities available. Along the riverbank, there are two {{ill|Outdoor Baths|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo|1=Rotenburo|ko|노천탕|1=Rotenburo|qq=draft}}; one of these is known as "Sentaku-yu" (Laundry Bath), where the practice of '''foot‑stepping laundry''' is famous. This method utilizes the drifting effect of the hot spring water—with a pH of 9.2—to aid in washing, a technique developed while keeping watch for bears and wolves in the surrounding mountains【湯の心旅】Okutsu Onsen (Okayama Prefecture) heralds spring with "foot‑stepping laundry" – washing away fatigue from within and without, as reported in the {{ill|Nikkei|ja|日本経済新聞|qq=draft}} morning edition, December 21, 2020 (separate printing, NIKKEI Plus 1, page 13)主婦の友社（編）『主婦の友ベストBooks 見直したい日本の「美」 日本の名湯100選』 p.135 主婦の友社 2010th year 3月10日発行 ISBN 978-4-07-270188-1. Today, some locals still use the baths for laundry, and live demonstrations for tourists are held on Sundays and public holidays from March to November. Along National Route 179, there is a roadside station, {{ill|Michinoeki Okutsu Onsen|ja|道の駅奥津温泉|qq=draft}}, though it does not include bathing facilities. Downstream, approximately 2 km from the laundry area along the Yoshii River, there are also {{ill|Han'nya-ji Onsen|ja|般若寺温泉|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Ōdzuri Onsen|ja|大釣温泉|qq=draft}}; these are believed to be sourced from the same fault line as the upstream Okutsu Onsen, and in some contexts the entire area is collectively referred to as "Okutsu Onsen"日本温泉科学会（監修）『図説 日本の温泉 ―170温泉のサイエンス―』 p.133 朝倉書店 2020th year 3月1日発行 ISBN 978-4-254-16075-8. == History == According to the {{ill|Onsen Origin Myth|lt=Hot Spring Founding Legend|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|qq=draft}}, the hot spring was discovered by {{ill|Sukunabikona-no-mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft}}. During the {{ill|Edo Period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft}}, it served as a site for hot spring treatment ({{ill|Tōji|ja|湯治|1=Thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|1=Thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|qq=draft}}) for the {{ill|Tsuyama Domain|ja|津山藩|qq=draft}}, and the area around the present-day foot‑stepping laundry is said to have once housed a "Locked Bath" ({{ill|Kagiyu|ja|鍵湯|qq=draft}}), from which usage was restricted by domain lord {{ill|Mori Tadamasu|ja|森忠政|qq=draft}}, who stationed guards to lock it. The cosmetics company {{ill|KOSÉ|ja|コーセー|qq=draft}} has developed a lotion based on the components of this hot spring. Foot‑stepping laundry has long been practiced; its process was even commemorated in a tanka by {{ill|Yosano Tekkan|ja|与謝野鉄幹|qq=draft}}:
衣洗う　奥津のおとめ　河床の　清きに立ちて　踊るごと践む
However, a large fire in 1926 destroyed the hot spring town山村 順次 『47都道府県・温泉百科』 p.213 丸善出版 2015th year 12月30日発行 ISBN 978-4-621-08996-5. The area is also known as the setting of {{ill|Akitsu Onsen|ja|秋津温泉|qq=draft}}, a novel by {{ill|Fujiwara Shini|ja|藤原審爾|qq=draft}}, which was later adapted into a film in 1962 that boosted the prominence of Okutsu Onsen. On August 1, 1966, Okutsu Onsen was designated a {{ill|National Recreational Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|qq=draft}}, and in 1991 it was designated as a {{ill|National Health Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保健温泉地|qq=draft}}. An express bus service, nicknamed the "Romance Car," operated by {{ill|Chūtetsu Bus|ja|中鉄バス|qq=draft}} connects {{ill|Okayama Station|ja|岡山駅|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tsuyama Station|ja|津山駅|qq=draft}}, and Okutsu Onsen (stopping in front of the roadside station on National Route 179) with one round trip per day. == Access == * '''Automobile''': Approximately 25 minutes by car from the {{ill|Chūgoku Expressway|ja|中国自動車道|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Inshō Interchange|ja|院庄インターチェンジ|qq=draft}}. * '''Railway''': Alight at {{ill|Tsuyama Station|ja|津山駅|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Himeshin Line|ja|姫新線|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tsuyama Line|ja|津山線|qq=draft}}), then take the bus from bus terminal stop #5 operated by {{ill|Chūtetsu Hokubu Bus|ja|中鉄北部バス|qq=draft}}: ** Board the Okutsu Onsen/Ishikoshi Line for about 50 minutes (operating 4 round trips per day; 2 round trips on weekends and holidays). ** Transfer to the {{ill|Gongo Bus|ja|ごんごバス|qq=draft}} West Loop line, and at either "Marunaka Inshō Store" or "Plant 5," transfer to a community bus bound for the Kamisaihara Promotion Center (operating 4 round trips per day). * '''Highway Bus''': From {{ill|Namba|ja|なんば|qq=draft}} (via the {{ill|Osaka City Air Terminal|ja|なんば|qq=draft}}) and from {{ill|Shin-Osaka Station|ja|新大阪駅|qq=draft}} (via the Hankyu High-Speed Bus Shin-Osaka Terminal at the North Exit), take the {{ill|Nikkō Kōsokubasu|ja|日交高速バス|qq=draft}} on the line from {{ill|Kurayoshi City|ja|倉吉市|qq=draft}} to {{ill|Kobe City|ja|神戸市|qq=draft}}/Osaka. Alight at Okutsu Onsen-guchi (in front of the roadside station on {{ill|National Route 179|ja|国道179号|qq=draft}}); there is one round trip per day. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{WikiPortal|Hot Springs}} * [[:en:List_of_hot_springs_in_Japan|List of Hot Spring Resorts in Japan]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Okutsu Onsen}} * [https://www.mto.ne.jp/okutsuonsenkk/ Okutsu Onsen Tourism Association] * [http://kanko.town.kagamino.lg.jp/hot_spring/okutsu-spa/ Kagamino Town Tourism Navi: Okutsu Onsen] * [https://afw-at.com/page.php?id=146 Okutsu Onsen] – At Town Web Magazine ''' ''' '''''' {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|奥津温泉|version=104384327|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot Springs in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreational Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Geography of Kagamino Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Legends]] [[Category:Onsen Origin Myth]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Un11]] }} [[sn:Okutsu Onsen]] [[ja:奥津温泉]] [[zh:温泉]] [[ja:嬉野温泉]] [[ja:武雄温泉]] [[ja:日本書紀]] [[ja:南紀白浜温泉]] [[ja:行基]] [[ja:空海]] [[ja:一遍]] [[ja:仏教]] [[ja:坂上田村麻呂]] [[ja:高野聖]] [[ja:大友頼泰]] [[ja:浜脇温泉]] [[ja:別府温泉]] [[ja:鉄輪温泉]] [[ja:火男火売神社]] [[ja:下呂温泉]] [[ja:山中温泉]] [[ja:道後温泉]] [[ja:城崎温泉]] [[ja:湯田温泉]] [[ja:三朝温泉]] [[ja:山鹿温泉]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] [[ja:老神温泉]] [[zh:温泉神社]] [[zh:温泉寺]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] [[en:Okutsu Onsen]]

Along National Route 179, there is a roadside station, {{ill|Michinoeki Okutsu Onsen|ja|道の駅奥津温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoeki Okutsu Onsen|qq=}}, though it does not include bathing facilities. Downstream, approximately 2 km from the laundry area along the Yoshii River, there are also {{ill|Han'nya-ji Onsen|ja|般若寺温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Han'nya-ji Onsen|qq=}} and {{ill|Ōdzuri Onsen|ja|大釣温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdzuri Onsen|qq=}}; these are believed to be sourced from the same fault line as the upstream Okutsu Onsen, and in some contexts the entire area is collectively referred to as "Okutsu Onsen"日本温泉科学会（監修）『図説 日本の温泉 ―170温泉のサイエンス―』 p.133 朝倉書店 2020th year 3月1日発行 ISBN 978-4-254-16075-8. == History == According to the {{ill|Onsen Origin Myth|lt=Hot Spring Founding Legend|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|qq=}}, the hot spring was discovered by {{ill|Sukunabikona-no-mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}}. During the {{ill|Edo Period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo Period|qq=}}, it served as a site for hot spring treatment ({{ill|Tōji|ja|湯治|1=Thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|1=Thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Thermal bath|qq=}}) for the {{ill|Tsuyama Domain|ja|津山藩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuyama Domain|qq=}}, and the area around the present-day foot‑stepping laundry is said to have once housed a "Locked Bath" ({{ill|Kagiyu|ja|鍵湯|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagiyu|qq=}}), from which usage was restricted by domain lord {{ill|Mori Tadamasu|ja|森忠政|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Tadamasu|qq=}}, who stationed guards to lock it. The cosmetics company {{ill|KOSÉ|ja|コーセー|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KOSÉ|qq=}} has developed a lotion based on the components of this hot spring. Foot‑stepping laundry has long been practiced; its process was even commemorated in a tanka by {{ill|Yosano Tekkan|ja|与謝野鉄幹|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yosano Tekkan|qq=}}:
衣洗う　奥津のおとめ　河床の　清きに立ちて　踊るごと践む
However, a large fire in 1926 destroyed the hot spring town山村 順次 『47都道府県・温泉百科』 p.213 丸善出版 2015th year 12月30日発行 ISBN 978-4-621-08996-5. The area is also known as the setting of {{ill|Akitsu Onsen|ja|秋津温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akitsu Onsen|qq=}}, a novel by {{ill|Fujiwara Shini|ja|藤原審爾|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Shini|qq=}}, which was later adapted into a film in 1962 that boosted the prominence of Okutsu Onsen. On August 1, 1966, Okutsu Onsen was designated a {{ill|National Recreational Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Recreational Hot Spring Resort|qq=}}, and in 1991 it was designated as a {{ill|National Health Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保健温泉地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Health Hot Spring Resort|qq=}}. An express bus service, nicknamed the "Romance Car," operated by {{ill|Chūtetsu Bus|ja|中鉄バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūtetsu Bus|qq=}} connects {{ill|Okayama Station|ja|岡山駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Station|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsuyama Station|ja|津山駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuyama Station|qq=}}, and Okutsu Onsen (stopping in front of the roadside station on National Route 179) with one round trip per day. == Access == * '''Automobile''': Approximately 25 minutes by car from the {{ill|Chūgoku Expressway|ja|中国自動車道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūgoku Expressway|qq=}} and {{ill|Inshō Interchange|ja|院庄インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inshō Interchange|qq=}}. * '''Railway''': Alight at {{ill|Tsuyama Station|ja|津山駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuyama Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR West|qq=}} ({{ill|Himeshin Line|ja|姫新線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himeshin Line|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsuyama Line|ja|津山線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuyama Line|qq=}}), then take the bus from bus terminal stop #5 operated by {{ill|Chūtetsu Hokubu Bus|ja|中鉄北部バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūtetsu Hokubu Bus|qq=}}: ** Board the Okutsu Onsen/Ishikoshi Line for about 50 minutes (operating 4 round trips per day; 2 round trips on weekends and holidays). ** Transfer to the {{ill|Gongo Bus|ja|ごんごバス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gongo Bus|qq=}} West Loop line, and at either "Marunaka Inshō Store" or "Plant 5," transfer to a community bus bound for the Kamisaihara Promotion Center (operating 4 round trips per day). * '''Highway Bus''': From {{ill|Namba|ja|なんば|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namba|qq=}} (via the {{ill|Osaka City Air Terminal|ja|なんば|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka City Air Terminal|qq=}}) and from {{ill|Shin-Osaka Station|ja|新大阪駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin-Osaka Station|qq=}} (via the Hankyu High-Speed Bus Shin-Osaka Terminal at the North Exit), take the {{ill|Nikkō Kōsokubasu|ja|日交高速バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō Kōsokubasu|qq=}} on the line from {{ill|Kurayoshi City|ja|倉吉市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayoshi City|qq=}} to {{ill|Kobe City|ja|神戸市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe City|qq=}}/Osaka. Alight at Okutsu Onsen-guchi (in front of the roadside station on {{ill|National Route 179|ja|国道179号|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Route 179|qq=}}); there is one round trip per day. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{WikiPortal|Hot Springs}} * [[:en:List_of_hot_springs_in_Japan|List of Hot Spring Resorts in Japan]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Okutsu Onsen}} * [https://www.mto.ne.jp/okutsuonsenkk/ Okutsu Onsen Tourism Association] * [http://kanko.town.kagamino.lg.jp/hot_spring/okutsu-spa/ Kagamino Town Tourism Navi: Okutsu Onsen] * [https://afw-at.com/page.php?id=146 Okutsu Onsen] – At Town Web Magazine ''' ''' '''''' {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|奥津温泉|version=104384327|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot Springs in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreational Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Geography of Kagamino Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Legends]] [[Category:Onsen Origin Myth]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Un11]] }} [[sn:Okutsu Onsen]] [[ja:奥津温泉]] [[zh:温泉]] [[ja:嬉野温泉]] [[ja:武雄温泉]] [[ja:日本書紀]] [[ja:南紀白浜温泉]] [[ja:行基]] [[ja:空海]] [[ja:一遍]] [[ja:仏教]] [[ja:坂上田村麻呂]] [[ja:高野聖]] [[ja:大友頼泰]] [[ja:浜脇温泉]] [[ja:別府温泉]] [[ja:鉄輪温泉]] [[ja:火男火売神社]] [[ja:下呂温泉]] [[ja:山中温泉]] [[ja:道後温泉]] [[ja:城崎温泉]] [[ja:湯田温泉]] [[ja:三朝温泉]] [[ja:山鹿温泉]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] [[ja:老神温泉]] [[zh:温泉神社]] [[zh:温泉寺]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] [[en:Okutsu Onsen]]

revision.text:
{{infobox Onsen |Name = Okutsu Onsen |image = Okutsu Onsen 03.jpg |Comment = Day-trip Hot Spring Facility "Hanabijin no Sato" |Location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Tomata District|ja|苫田郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomata District|qq=}} {{ill|Kagamino Town|ja|鏡野町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagamino Town|qq=}} {{Location map many | Japan Okayama Prefecture | relief = 1 | caption = | width = 220 | float = center | lat_deg = 35.2229 | lon_deg = 133.9201 | pos = top | label = '''Okutsu Onsen''' | lat2_deg = 35.0547 | lon2_deg = 134.0030 | mark2 = blue pog.svg | pos2 = right | label2 = {{ill|Tsuyama Station|ja|津山駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuyama Station|qq=}} | lat3_deg = 35.0624 | lon3_deg = 133.9495 | mark3 = Turquoise pog.svg | pos3 = left | label3 = {{ill|Inshō Interchange|ja|院庄I.C.|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inshō Interchange|qq=}} | lat4_deg = 34.6663 | lon4_deg = 133.9185 | mark4 = blue pog.svg | pos4 = right | label4 = {{ill|Okayama Station|ja|岡山駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Station|qq=}} }}
Map of Okayama Prefecture
|Latitude Degrees = 35 |Latitude Minutes = 13 |Latitude Seconds = 22.7 | N/S = N |Longitude Degrees = 133 |Longitude Minutes = 55 |Longitude Seconds = 12.6 | E/W = E |Access = Railway: {{ill|Himeshin Line|ja|姫新線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himeshin Line|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsuyama Line|ja|津山線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuyama Line|qq=}} – from {{ill|Tsuyama Station|ja|津山駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuyama Station|qq=}}, take {{ill|Chūtetsu Hokubu Bus|ja|中鉄北部バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūtetsu Hokubu Bus|qq=}} for about 50 minutes |Water Type = {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} |Temperature = 35 - 44 |Discharge = 1000 L (per minute) |pH = |Liquid Classification = {{ill|Alkaline|ja|アルカリ性|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alkaline|qq=}} |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Accommodations = |Total Capacity = |Annual Bath Visitors = |Statistical Year = |External links = [http://kanko.town.kagamino.lg.jp/hot_spring/okutsu-spa/ Kagamino Town Tourism Navi: Okutsu Onsen] |Special Notes = }} '''Okutsu Onsen''' (おくつ温泉) is a hot spring that emerges in {{ill|Kagamino Town|ja|鏡野町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagamino Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture|qq=}}. Located in the area formerly known as {{ill|Mimasaka Province|1=Mimasaka Province|2=ja|3=美作国|ja|美作国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimasaka Province|qq=}}, it is one of the "Mimasaka Three Hot Springs," together with [[:en:Yunogo_Onsen|Yunogo Onsen]] and {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|ceb|Yubara hotspring|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|qq=}}. == Water Quality == * {{ill|Alkaline Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alkaline Simple Hot Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} – Known as the "Hot Spring for Beauties"主婦の友社（編）『主婦の友ベストBooks 見直したい日本の「美」 日本の名湯100選』 p.134 主婦の友社 2010th year 3月10日発行 ISBN 978-4-07-270188-1. ** Source temperature: 35 - 44 ℃ ** Discharge: 1000 (L/min) == Onsen Town == [[File:Okutsu Onsen 02.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Footbath area]] A hot spring town is formed around the Okutsu Bridge spanning the {{ill|Yoshii River|ja|吉井川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshii River|qq=}}, with day-trip bathing facilities available. Along the riverbank, there are two {{ill|Outdoor Baths|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo|1=Rotenburo|ko|노천탕|1=Rotenburo|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outdoor Baths|13=User:Immanuelle/Rotenburo|qq=}}; one of these is known as "Sentaku-yu" (Laundry Bath), where the practice of '''foot‑stepping laundry''' is famous. This method utilizes the drifting effect of the hot spring water—with a pH of 9.2—to aid in washing, a technique developed while keeping watch for bears and wolves in the surrounding mountains【湯の心旅】Okutsu Onsen (Okayama Prefecture) heralds spring with "foot‑stepping laundry" – washing away fatigue from within and without, as reported in the {{ill|Nikkei|ja|日本経済新聞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkei|qq=}} morning edition, December 21, 2020 (separate printing, NIKKEI Plus 1, page 13)主婦の友社（編）『主婦の友ベストBooks 見直したい日本の「美」 日本の名湯100選』 p.135 主婦の友社 2010th year 3月10日発行 ISBN 978-4-07-270188-1. Today, some locals still use the baths for laundry, and live demonstrations for tourists are held on Sundays and public holidays from March to November. Along National Route 179, there is a roadside station, {{ill|Michinoeki Okutsu Onsen|ja|道の駅奥津温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoeki Okutsu Onsen|qq=}}, though it does not include bathing facilities. Downstream, approximately 2 km from the laundry area along the Yoshii River, there are also {{ill|Han'nya-ji Onsen|ja|般若寺温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Han'nya-ji Onsen|qq=}} and {{ill|Ōdzuri Onsen|ja|大釣温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdzuri Onsen|qq=}}; these are believed to be sourced from the same fault line as the upstream Okutsu Onsen, and in some contexts the entire area is collectively referred to as "Okutsu Onsen"日本温泉科学会（監修）『図説 日本の温泉 ―170温泉のサイエンス―』 p.133 朝倉書店 2020th year 3月1日発行 ISBN 978-4-254-16075-8. == History == According to the {{ill|Onsen Origin Myth|lt=Hot Spring Founding Legend|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|qq=}}, the hot spring was discovered by {{ill|Sukunabikona-no-mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}}. During the {{ill|Edo Period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo Period|qq=}}, it served as a site for hot spring treatment ({{ill|Tōji|ja|湯治|1=Thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|1=Thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Thermal bath|qq=}}) for the {{ill|Tsuyama Domain|ja|津山藩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuyama Domain|qq=}}, and the area around the present-day foot‑stepping laundry is said to have once housed a "Locked Bath" ({{ill|Kagiyu|ja|鍵湯|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagiyu|qq=}}), from which usage was restricted by domain lord {{ill|Mori Tadamasu|ja|森忠政|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Tadamasu|qq=}}, who stationed guards to lock it. The cosmetics company {{ill|KOSÉ|ja|コーセー|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KOSÉ|qq=}} has developed a lotion based on the components of this hot spring. Foot‑stepping laundry has long been practiced; its process was even commemorated in a tanka by {{ill|Yosano Tekkan|ja|与謝野鉄幹|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yosano Tekkan|qq=}}:
衣洗う　奥津のおとめ　河床の　清きに立ちて　踊るごと践む
However, a large fire in 1926 destroyed the hot spring town山村 順次 『47都道府県・温泉百科』 p.213 丸善出版 2015th year 12月30日発行 ISBN 978-4-621-08996-5. The area is also known as the setting of {{ill|Akitsu Onsen|ja|秋津温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akitsu Onsen|qq=}}, a novel by {{ill|Fujiwara Shini|ja|藤原審爾|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Shini|qq=}}, which was later adapted into a film in 1962 that boosted the prominence of Okutsu Onsen. On August 1, 1966, Okutsu Onsen was designated a {{ill|National Recreational Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Recreational Hot Spring Resort|qq=}}, and in 1991 it was designated as a {{ill|National Health Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保健温泉地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Health Hot Spring Resort|qq=}}. An express bus service, nicknamed the "Romance Car," operated by {{ill|Chūtetsu Bus|ja|中鉄バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūtetsu Bus|qq=}} connects {{ill|Okayama Station|ja|岡山駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Station|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsuyama Station|ja|津山駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuyama Station|qq=}}, and Okutsu Onsen (stopping in front of the roadside station on National Route 179) with one round trip per day. == Access == * '''Automobile''': Approximately 25 minutes by car from the {{ill|Chūgoku Expressway|ja|中国自動車道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūgoku Expressway|qq=}} and {{ill|Inshō Interchange|ja|院庄インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inshō Interchange|qq=}}. * '''Railway''': Alight at {{ill|Tsuyama Station|ja|津山駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuyama Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR West|qq=}} ({{ill|Himeshin Line|ja|姫新線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himeshin Line|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsuyama Line|ja|津山線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuyama Line|qq=}}), then take the bus from bus terminal stop #5 operated by {{ill|Chūtetsu Hokubu Bus|ja|中鉄北部バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūtetsu Hokubu Bus|qq=}}: ** Board the Okutsu Onsen/Ishikoshi Line for about 50 minutes (operating 4 round trips per day; 2 round trips on weekends and holidays). ** Transfer to the {{ill|Gongo Bus|ja|ごんごバス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gongo Bus|qq=}} West Loop line, and at either "Marunaka Inshō Store" or "Plant 5," transfer to a community bus bound for the Kamisaihara Promotion Center (operating 4 round trips per day). * '''Highway Bus''': From {{ill|Namba|ja|なんば|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namba|qq=}} (via the {{ill|Osaka City Air Terminal|ja|なんば|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka City Air Terminal|qq=}}) and from {{ill|Shin-Osaka Station|ja|新大阪駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin-Osaka Station|qq=}} (via the Hankyu High-Speed Bus Shin-Osaka Terminal at the North Exit), take the {{ill|Nikkō Kōsokubasu|ja|日交高速バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō Kōsokubasu|qq=}} on the line from {{ill|Kurayoshi City|ja|倉吉市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayoshi City|qq=}} to {{ill|Kobe City|ja|神戸市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe City|qq=}}/Osaka. Alight at Okutsu Onsen-guchi (in front of the roadside station on {{ill|National Route 179|ja|国道179号|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Route 179|qq=}}); there is one round trip per day. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{WikiPortal|Hot Springs}} * [[:en:List_of_hot_springs_in_Japan|List of Hot Spring Resorts in Japan]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Okutsu Onsen}} * [https://www.mto.ne.jp/okutsuonsenkk/ Okutsu Onsen Tourism Association] * [http://kanko.town.kagamino.lg.jp/hot_spring/okutsu-spa/ Kagamino Town Tourism Navi: Okutsu Onsen] * [https://afw-at.com/page.php?id=146 Okutsu Onsen] – At Town Web Magazine ''' ''' '''''' {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|奥津温泉|version=104384327|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot Springs in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreational Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Geography of Kagamino Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Legends]] [[Category:Onsen Origin Myth]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Un11]] }} [[sn:Okutsu Onsen]] [[ja:奥津温泉]] [[zh:温泉]] [[ja:嬉野温泉]] [[ja:武雄温泉]] [[ja:日本書紀]] [[ja:南紀白浜温泉]] [[ja:行基]] [[ja:空海]] [[ja:一遍]] [[ja:仏教]] [[ja:坂上田村麻呂]] [[ja:高野聖]] [[ja:大友頼泰]] [[ja:浜脇温泉]] [[ja:別府温泉]] [[ja:鉄輪温泉]] [[ja:火男火売神社]] [[ja:下呂温泉]] [[ja:山中温泉]] [[ja:道後温泉]] [[ja:城崎温泉]] [[ja:湯田温泉]] [[ja:三朝温泉]] [[ja:山鹿温泉]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] [[ja:老神温泉]] [[zh:温泉神社]] [[zh:温泉寺]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] [[en:Okutsu Onsen]]

|Latitude Degrees = 35 |Latitude Minutes = 13 |Latitude Seconds = 22.7 | N/S = N |Longitude Degrees = 133 |Longitude Minutes = 55 |Longitude Seconds = 12.6 | E/W = E |Access = Railway: [[:en:Kishin Line|Himeshin Line]], [[:en:Tsuyama Line|Tsuyama Line]] – from [[:en:Tsuyama Station|Tsuyama Station]], take {{ill|Chūtetsu Hokubu Bus|ja|中鉄北部バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūtetsu Hokubu Bus|qq=}} for about 50 minutes |Water Type = {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} |Temperature = 35 - 44 |Discharge = 1000 L (per minute) |pH = |Liquid Classification = {{ill|Alkaline|ja|アルカリ性|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alkaline|qq=}} |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Accommodations = |Total Capacity = |Annual Bath Visitors = |Statistical Year = |External links = [http://kanko.town.kagamino.lg.jp/hot_spring/okutsu-spa/ Kagamino Town Tourism Navi: Okutsu Onsen] |Special Notes = }} '''Okutsu Onsen''' (おくつ温泉) is a hot spring that emerges in [[:en:Kagamino|Kagamino Town]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. Located in the area formerly known as [[:en:Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka Province]], it is one of the "Mimasaka Three Hot Springs," together with [[:en:Yunogo_Onsen|Yunogo Onsen]] and {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|ceb|Yubara hotspring|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|qq=}}. == Water Quality == * {{ill|Alkaline Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alkaline Simple Hot Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} – Known as the "Hot Spring for Beauties"主婦の友社（編）『主婦の友ベストBooks 見直したい日本の「美」 日本の名湯100選』 p.134 主婦の友社 2010th year 3月10日発行 ISBN 978-4-07-270188-1. ** Source temperature: 35 - 44 ℃ ** Discharge: 1000 (L/min) == Onsen Town == [[File:Okutsu Onsen 02.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Footbath area]] A hot spring town is formed around the Okutsu Bridge spanning the [[:en:Yoshii River|Yoshii River]], with day-trip bathing facilities available. Along the riverbank, there are two {{ill|Outdoor Baths|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo|1=Rotenburo|ko|노천탕|1=Rotenburo|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outdoor Baths|13=User:Immanuelle/Rotenburo|qq=}}; one of these is known as "Sentaku-yu" (Laundry Bath), where the practice of '''foot‑stepping laundry''' is famous. This method utilizes the drifting effect of the hot spring water—with a pH of 9.2—to aid in washing, a technique developed while keeping watch for bears and wolves in the surrounding mountains【湯の心旅】Okutsu Onsen (Okayama Prefecture) heralds spring with "foot‑stepping laundry" – washing away fatigue from within and without, as reported in the [[:en:The Nikkei|Nikkei]] morning edition, December 21, 2020 (separate printing, NIKKEI Plus 1, page 13)主婦の友社（編）『主婦の友ベストBooks 見直したい日本の「美」 日本の名湯100選』 p.135 主婦の友社 2010th year 3月10日発行 ISBN 978-4-07-270188-1. Today, some locals still use the baths for laundry, and live demonstrations for tourists are held on Sundays and public holidays from March to November. Along National Route 179, there is a roadside station, {{ill|Michinoeki Okutsu Onsen|ja|道の駅奥津温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoeki Okutsu Onsen|qq=}}, though it does not include bathing facilities. Downstream, approximately 2 km from the laundry area along the Yoshii River, there are also {{ill|Han'nya-ji Onsen|ja|般若寺温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Han'nya-ji Onsen|qq=}} and {{ill|Ōdzuri Onsen|ja|大釣温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdzuri Onsen|qq=}}; these are believed to be sourced from the same fault line as the upstream Okutsu Onsen, and in some contexts the entire area is collectively referred to as "Okutsu Onsen"日本温泉科学会（監修）『図説 日本の温泉 ―170温泉のサイエンス―』 p.133 朝倉書店 2020th year 3月1日発行 ISBN 978-4-254-16075-8. == History == According to the {{ill|Onsen Origin Myth|lt=Hot Spring Founding Legend|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|qq=}}, the hot spring was discovered by {{ill|Sukunabikona-no-mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo Period]], it served as a site for hot spring treatment ({{ill|Tōji|ja|湯治|1=Thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|1=Thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Thermal bath|qq=}}) for the [[:en:Tsuyama Domain|Tsuyama Domain]], and the area around the present-day foot‑stepping laundry is said to have once housed a "Locked Bath" ({{ill|Kagiyu|ja|鍵湯|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagiyu|qq=}}), from which usage was restricted by domain lord {{ill|Mori Tadamasu|ja|森忠政|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Tadamasu|qq=}}, who stationed guards to lock it. The cosmetics company [[:en:Kosé|KOSÉ]] has developed a lotion based on the components of this hot spring. Foot‑stepping laundry has long been practiced; its process was even commemorated in a tanka by [[:en:Tekkan Yosano|Yosano Tekkan]]:
衣洗う　奥津のおとめ　河床の　清きに立ちて　踊るごと践む
However, a large fire in 1926 destroyed the hot spring town山村 順次 『47都道府県・温泉百科』 p.213 丸善出版 2015th year 12月30日発行 ISBN 978-4-621-08996-5. The area is also known as the setting of [[:en:Akitsu Springs|Akitsu Onsen]], a novel by {{ill|Fujiwara Shini|ja|藤原審爾|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Shini|qq=}}, which was later adapted into a film in 1962 that boosted the prominence of Okutsu Onsen. On August 1, 1966, Okutsu Onsen was designated a {{ill|National Recreational Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Recreational Hot Spring Resort|qq=}}, and in 1991 it was designated as a {{ill|National Health Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保健温泉地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Health Hot Spring Resort|qq=}}. An express bus service, nicknamed the "Romance Car," operated by {{ill|Chūtetsu Bus|ja|中鉄バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūtetsu Bus|qq=}} connects [[:en:Okayama Station|Okayama Station]], [[:en:Tsuyama Station|Tsuyama Station]], and Okutsu Onsen (stopping in front of the roadside station on National Route 179) with one round trip per day. == Access == * '''Automobile''': Approximately 25 minutes by car from the [[:en:Chūgoku Expressway|Chūgoku Expressway]] and {{ill|Inshō Interchange|ja|院庄インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inshō Interchange|qq=}}. * '''Railway''': Alight at [[:en:Tsuyama Station|Tsuyama Station]] on the {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR West|qq=}} ([[:en:Kishin Line|Himeshin Line]], [[:en:Tsuyama Line|Tsuyama Line]]), then take the bus from bus terminal stop #5 operated by {{ill|Chūtetsu Hokubu Bus|ja|中鉄北部バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūtetsu Hokubu Bus|qq=}}: ** Board the Okutsu Onsen/Ishikoshi Line for about 50 minutes (operating 4 round trips per day; 2 round trips on weekends and holidays). ** Transfer to the {{ill|Gongo Bus|ja|ごんごバス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gongo Bus|qq=}} West Loop line, and at either "Marunaka Inshō Store" or "Plant 5," transfer to a community bus bound for the Kamisaihara Promotion Center (operating 4 round trips per day). * '''Highway Bus''': From {{ill|Namba|ja|なんば|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namba|qq=}} (via the {{ill|Osaka City Air Terminal|ja|なんば|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka City Air Terminal|qq=}}) and from [[:en:Shin-Ōsaka Station|Shin-Osaka Station]] (via the Hankyu High-Speed Bus Shin-Osaka Terminal at the North Exit), take the {{ill|Nikkō Kōsokubasu|ja|日交高速バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō Kōsokubasu|qq=}} on the line from [[:en:Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi City]] to [[:en:Kobe|Kobe City]]/Osaka. Alight at Okutsu Onsen-guchi (in front of the roadside station on [[:en:Japan National Route 179|National Route 179]]); there is one round trip per day. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{WikiPortal|Hot Springs}} * [[:en:List_of_hot_springs_in_Japan|List of Hot Spring Resorts in Japan]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Okutsu Onsen}} * [https://www.mto.ne.jp/okutsuonsenkk/ Okutsu Onsen Tourism Association] * [http://kanko.town.kagamino.lg.jp/hot_spring/okutsu-spa/ Kagamino Town Tourism Navi: Okutsu Onsen] * [https://afw-at.com/page.php?id=146 Okutsu Onsen] – At Town Web Magazine ''' ''' '''''' {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|奥津温泉|version=104384327|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot Springs in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreational Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Geography of Kagamino Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Legends]] [[Category:Onsen Origin Myth]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Un11]] }} [[sn:Okutsu Onsen]] [[ja:奥津温泉]] [[zh:温泉]] [[ja:嬉野温泉]] [[ja:武雄温泉]] [[ja:日本書紀]] [[ja:南紀白浜温泉]] [[ja:行基]] [[ja:空海]] [[ja:一遍]] [[ja:仏教]] [[ja:坂上田村麻呂]] [[ja:高野聖]] [[ja:大友頼泰]] [[ja:浜脇温泉]] [[ja:別府温泉]] [[ja:鉄輪温泉]] [[ja:火男火売神社]] [[ja:下呂温泉]] [[ja:山中温泉]] [[ja:道後温泉]] [[ja:城崎温泉]] [[ja:湯田温泉]] [[ja:三朝温泉]] [[ja:山鹿温泉]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] [[ja:老神温泉]] [[zh:温泉神社]] [[zh:温泉寺]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] [[en:Okutsu Onsen]]

Downstream, approximately 2 km from the laundry area along the Yoshii River, there are also {{ill|Han'nya-ji Onsen|ja|般若寺温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Han'nya-ji Onsen|qq=}} and {{ill|Ōdzuri Onsen|ja|大釣温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdzuri Onsen|qq=}}; these are believed to be sourced from the same fault line as the upstream Okutsu Onsen, and in some contexts the entire area is collectively referred to as "Okutsu Onsen"日本温泉科学会（監修）『図説 日本の温泉 ―170温泉のサイエンス―』 p.133 朝倉書店 2020th year 3月1日発行 ISBN 978-4-254-16075-8. == History == According to the {{ill|Onsen Origin Myth|lt=Hot Spring Founding Legend|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|qq=}}, the hot spring was discovered by {{ill|Sukunabikona-no-mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}}. The cosmetics company [[:en:Kosé|KOSÉ]] has developed a lotion based on the components of this hot spring. However, a large fire in 1926 destroyed the hot spring town山村 順次 『47都道府県・温泉百科』 p.213 丸善出版 2015th year 12月30日発行 ISBN 978-4-621-08996-5. The area is also known as the setting of [[:en:Akitsu Springs|Akitsu Onsen]], a novel by {{ill|Fujiwara Shini|ja|藤原審爾|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Shini|qq=}}, which was later adapted into a film in 1962 that boosted the prominence of Okutsu Onsen. On August 1, 1966, Okutsu Onsen was designated a {{ill|National Recreational Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Recreational Hot Spring Resort|qq=}}, and in 1991 it was designated as a {{ill|National Health Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保健温泉地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Health Hot Spring Resort|qq=}}. An express bus service, nicknamed the "Romance Car," operated by {{ill|Chūtetsu Bus|ja|中鉄バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūtetsu Bus|qq=}} connects [[:en:Okayama Station|Okayama Station]], [[:en:Tsuyama Station|Tsuyama Station]], and Okutsu Onsen (stopping in front of the roadside station on National Route 179) with one round trip per day. == Access == * '''Automobile''': Approximately 25 minutes by car from the [[:en:Chūgoku Expressway|Chūgoku Expressway]] and {{ill|Inshō Interchange|ja|院庄インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inshō Interchange|qq=}}. * '''Railway''': Alight at [[:en:Tsuyama Station|Tsuyama Station]] on the {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR West|qq=}} ([[:en:Kishin Line|Himeshin Line]], [[:en:Tsuyama Line|Tsuyama Line]]), then take the bus from bus terminal stop #5 operated by {{ill|Chūtetsu Hokubu Bus|ja|中鉄北部バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūtetsu Hokubu Bus|qq=}}: ** Board the Okutsu Onsen/Ishikoshi Line for about 50 minutes (operating 4 round trips per day; 2 round trips on weekends and holidays). ** Transfer to the {{ill|Gongo Bus|ja|ごんごバス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gongo Bus|qq=}} West Loop line, and at either "Marunaka Inshō Store" or "Plant 5," transfer to a community bus bound for the Kamisaihara Promotion Center (operating 4 round trips per day). * '''Highway Bus''': From {{ill|Namba|ja|なんば|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namba|qq=}} (via the {{ill|Osaka City Air Terminal|ja|なんば|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka City Air Terminal|qq=}}) and from [[:en:Shin-Ōsaka Station|Shin-Osaka Station]] (via the Hankyu High-Speed Bus Shin-Osaka Terminal at the North Exit), take the {{ill|Nikkō Kōsokubasu|ja|日交高速バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō Kōsokubasu|qq=}} on the line from [[:en:Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi City]] to [[:en:Kobe|Kobe City]]/Osaka. Alight at Okutsu Onsen-guchi (in front of the roadside station on [[:en:Japan National Route 179|National Route 179]]); there is one round trip per day. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{WikiPortal|Hot Springs}} * [[:en:List_of_hot_springs_in_Japan|List of Hot Spring Resorts in Japan]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Okutsu Onsen}} * [https://www.mto.ne.jp/okutsuonsenkk/ Okutsu Onsen Tourism Association] * [http://kanko.town.kagamino.lg.jp/hot_spring/okutsu-spa/ Kagamino Town Tourism Navi: Okutsu Onsen] * [https://afw-at.com/page.php?id=146 Okutsu Onsen] – At Town Web Magazine ''' ''' '''''' {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|奥津温泉|version=104384327|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot Springs in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreational Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Geography of Kagamino Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Legends]] [[Category:Onsen Origin Myth]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Un11]] }} [[sn:Okutsu Onsen]] [[ja:奥津温泉]] [[zh:温泉]] [[ja:嬉野温泉]] [[ja:武雄温泉]] [[ja:日本書紀]] [[ja:南紀白浜温泉]] [[ja:行基]] [[ja:空海]] [[ja:一遍]] [[ja:仏教]] [[ja:坂上田村麻呂]] [[ja:高野聖]] [[ja:大友頼泰]] [[ja:浜脇温泉]] [[ja:別府温泉]] [[ja:鉄輪温泉]] [[ja:火男火売神社]] [[ja:下呂温泉]] [[ja:山中温泉]] [[ja:道後温泉]] [[ja:城崎温泉]] [[ja:湯田温泉]] [[ja:三朝温泉]] [[ja:山鹿温泉]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] [[ja:老神温泉]] [[zh:温泉神社]] [[zh:温泉寺]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] [[en:Okutsu Onsen]] [[Category:国民保養温泉地]]

revision.text:
{{infobox Onsen |Name = Okutsu Onsen |image = Okutsu Onsen 03.jpg |Comment = Day-trip Hot Spring Facility "Hanabijin no Sato" |Location = [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]] [[:en:Tomata District, Okayama|Tomata District]] [[:en:Kagamino|Kagamino Town]] {{Location map many | Japan Okayama Prefecture | relief = 1 | caption = | width = 220 | float = center | lat_deg = 35.2229 | lon_deg = 133.9201 | pos = top | label = '''Okutsu Onsen''' | lat2_deg = 35.0547 | lon2_deg = 134.0030 | mark2 = blue pog.svg | pos2 = right | label2 = [[:en:Tsuyama Station|Tsuyama Station]] | lat3_deg = 35.0624 | lon3_deg = 133.9495 | mark3 = Turquoise pog.svg | pos3 = left | label3 = {{ill|Inshō Interchange|ja|院庄I.C.|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inshō Interchange|qq=}} | lat4_deg = 34.6663 | lon4_deg = 133.9185 | mark4 = blue pog.svg | pos4 = right | label4 = [[:en:Okayama Station|Okayama Station]] }}
Map of Okayama Prefecture
|Latitude Degrees = 35 |Latitude Minutes = 13 |Latitude Seconds = 22.7 | N/S = N |Longitude Degrees = 133 |Longitude Minutes = 55 |Longitude Seconds = 12.6 | E/W = E |Access = Railway: [[:en:Kishin Line|Himeshin Line]], [[:en:Tsuyama Line|Tsuyama Line]] – from [[:en:Tsuyama Station|Tsuyama Station]], take {{ill|Chūtetsu Hokubu Bus|ja|中鉄北部バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūtetsu Hokubu Bus|qq=}} for about 50 minutes |Water Type = {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} |Temperature = 35 - 44 |Discharge = 1000 L (per minute) |pH = |Liquid Classification = {{ill|Alkaline|ja|アルカリ性|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alkaline|qq=}} |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Accommodations = |Total Capacity = |Annual Bath Visitors = |Statistical Year = |External links = [http://kanko.town.kagamino.lg.jp/hot_spring/okutsu-spa/ Kagamino Town Tourism Navi: Okutsu Onsen] |Special Notes = }} '''Okutsu Onsen''' (おくつ温泉) is a hot spring that emerges in [[:en:Kagamino|Kagamino Town]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. Located in the area formerly known as [[:en:Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka Province]], it is one of the "Mimasaka Three Hot Springs," together with [[:en:Yunogo_Onsen|Yunogo Onsen]] and {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|ceb|Yubara hotspring|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|qq=}}. == Water Quality == * {{ill|Alkaline Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alkaline Simple Hot Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} – Known as the "Hot Spring for Beauties"主婦の友社（編）『主婦の友ベストBooks 見直したい日本の「美」 日本の名湯100選』 p.134 主婦の友社 2010th year 3月10日発行 ISBN 978-4-07-270188-1. ** Source temperature: 35 - 44 ℃ ** Discharge: 1000 (L/min) == Onsen Town == [[File:Okutsu Onsen 02.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Footbath area]] A hot spring town is formed around the Okutsu Bridge spanning the [[:en:Yoshii River|Yoshii River]], with day-trip bathing facilities available. Along the riverbank, there are two {{ill|Outdoor Baths|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo|1=Rotenburo|ko|노천탕|1=Rotenburo|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outdoor Baths|13=User:Immanuelle/Rotenburo|qq=}}; one of these is known as "Sentaku-yu" (Laundry Bath), where the practice of '''foot‑stepping laundry''' is famous. This method utilizes the drifting effect of the hot spring water—with a pH of 9.2—to aid in washing, a technique developed while keeping watch for bears and wolves in the surrounding mountains【湯の心旅】Okutsu Onsen (Okayama Prefecture) heralds spring with "foot‑stepping laundry" – washing away fatigue from within and without, as reported in the [[:en:The Nikkei|Nikkei]] morning edition, December 21, 2020 (separate printing, NIKKEI Plus 1, page 13)主婦の友社（編）『主婦の友ベストBooks 見直したい日本の「美」 日本の名湯100選』 p.135 主婦の友社 2010th year 3月10日発行 ISBN 978-4-07-270188-1. Today, some locals still use the baths for laundry, and live demonstrations for tourists are held on Sundays and public holidays from March to November. Along National Route 179, there is a roadside station, {{ill|Michinoeki Okutsu Onsen|ja|道の駅奥津温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoeki Okutsu Onsen|qq=}}, though it does not include bathing facilities. Downstream, approximately 2 km from the laundry area along the Yoshii River, there are also {{ill|Han'nya-ji Onsen|ja|般若寺温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Han'nya-ji Onsen|qq=}} and {{ill|Ōdzuri Onsen|ja|大釣温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdzuri Onsen|qq=}}; these are believed to be sourced from the same fault line as the upstream Okutsu Onsen, and in some contexts the entire area is collectively referred to as "Okutsu Onsen"日本温泉科学会（監修）『図説 日本の温泉 ―170温泉のサイエンス―』 p.133 朝倉書店 2020th year 3月1日発行 ISBN 978-4-254-16075-8. == History == According to the {{ill|Onsen Origin Myth|lt=Hot Spring Founding Legend|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|qq=}}, the hot spring was discovered by {{ill|Sukunabikona-no-mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo Period]], it served as a site for hot spring treatment ({{ill|Tōji|ja|湯治|1=Thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|1=Thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Thermal bath|qq=}}) for the [[:en:Tsuyama Domain|Tsuyama Domain]], and the area around the present-day foot‑stepping laundry is said to have once housed a "Locked Bath" ({{ill|Kagiyu|ja|鍵湯|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagiyu|qq=}}), from which usage was restricted by domain lord {{ill|Mori Tadamasu|ja|森忠政|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Tadamasu|qq=}}, who stationed guards to lock it. The cosmetics company [[:en:Kosé|KOSÉ]] has developed a lotion based on the components of this hot spring. Foot‑stepping laundry has long been practiced; its process was even commemorated in a tanka by [[:en:Tekkan Yosano|Yosano Tekkan]]:
衣洗う　奥津のおとめ　河床の　清きに立ちて　踊るごと践む
However, a large fire in 1926 destroyed the hot spring town山村 順次 『47都道府県・温泉百科』 p.213 丸善出版 2015th year 12月30日発行 ISBN 978-4-621-08996-5. The area is also known as the setting of [[:en:Akitsu Springs|Akitsu Onsen]], a novel by {{ill|Fujiwara Shini|ja|藤原審爾|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Shini|qq=}}, which was later adapted into a film in 1962 that boosted the prominence of Okutsu Onsen. On August 1, 1966, Okutsu Onsen was designated a {{ill|National Recreational Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Recreational Hot Spring Resort|qq=}}, and in 1991 it was designated as a {{ill|National Health Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保健温泉地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Health Hot Spring Resort|qq=}}. An express bus service, nicknamed the "Romance Car," operated by {{ill|Chūtetsu Bus|ja|中鉄バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūtetsu Bus|qq=}} connects [[:en:Okayama Station|Okayama Station]], [[:en:Tsuyama Station|Tsuyama Station]], and Okutsu Onsen (stopping in front of the roadside station on National Route 179) with one round trip per day. == Access == * '''Automobile''': Approximately 25 minutes by car from the [[:en:Chūgoku Expressway|Chūgoku Expressway]] and {{ill|Inshō Interchange|ja|院庄インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inshō Interchange|qq=}}. * '''Railway''': Alight at [[:en:Tsuyama Station|Tsuyama Station]] on the {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR West|qq=}} ([[:en:Kishin Line|Himeshin Line]], [[:en:Tsuyama Line|Tsuyama Line]]), then take the bus from bus terminal stop #5 operated by {{ill|Chūtetsu Hokubu Bus|ja|中鉄北部バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūtetsu Hokubu Bus|qq=}}: ** Board the Okutsu Onsen/Ishikoshi Line for about 50 minutes (operating 4 round trips per day; 2 round trips on weekends and holidays). ** Transfer to the {{ill|Gongo Bus|ja|ごんごバス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gongo Bus|qq=}} West Loop line, and at either "Marunaka Inshō Store" or "Plant 5," transfer to a community bus bound for the Kamisaihara Promotion Center (operating 4 round trips per day). * '''Highway Bus''': From {{ill|Namba|ja|なんば|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namba|qq=}} (via the {{ill|Osaka City Air Terminal|ja|なんば|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka City Air Terminal|qq=}}) and from [[:en:Shin-Ōsaka Station|Shin-Osaka Station]] (via the Hankyu High-Speed Bus Shin-Osaka Terminal at the North Exit), take the {{ill|Nikkō Kōsokubasu|ja|日交高速バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō Kōsokubasu|qq=}} on the line from [[:en:Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi City]] to [[:en:Kobe|Kobe City]]/Osaka. Alight at Okutsu Onsen-guchi (in front of the roadside station on [[:en:Japan National Route 179|National Route 179]]); there is one round trip per day. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{WikiPortal|Hot Springs}} * [[:en:List_of_hot_springs_in_Japan|List of Hot Spring Resorts in Japan]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Okutsu Onsen}} * [https://www.mto.ne.jp/okutsuonsenkk/ Okutsu Onsen Tourism Association] * [http://kanko.town.kagamino.lg.jp/hot_spring/okutsu-spa/ Kagamino Town Tourism Navi: Okutsu Onsen] * [https://afw-at.com/page.php?id=146 Okutsu Onsen] – At Town Web Magazine ''' ''' '''''' {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|奥津温泉|version=104384327|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot Springs in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreational Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Geography of Kagamino Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Legends]] [[Category:Onsen Origin Myth]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Un11]] }} [[sn:Okutsu Onsen]] [[ja:奥津温泉]] [[zh:温泉]] [[ja:嬉野温泉]] [[ja:武雄温泉]] [[ja:日本書紀]] [[ja:南紀白浜温泉]] [[ja:行基]] [[ja:空海]] [[ja:一遍]] [[ja:仏教]] [[ja:坂上田村麻呂]] [[ja:高野聖]] [[ja:大友頼泰]] [[ja:浜脇温泉]] [[ja:別府温泉]] [[ja:鉄輪温泉]] [[ja:火男火売神社]] [[ja:下呂温泉]] [[ja:山中温泉]] [[ja:道後温泉]] [[ja:城崎温泉]] [[ja:湯田温泉]] [[ja:三朝温泉]] [[ja:山鹿温泉]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] [[ja:老神温泉]] [[zh:温泉神社]] [[zh:温泉寺]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] [[en:Okutsu Onsen]] [[Category:国民保養温泉地]] [[Category:Hot springs of Okayama Prefecture]]

Along National Route 179, there is a roadside station, {{ill|Michinoeki Okutsu Onsen|ja|道の駅奥津温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoeki Okutsu Onsen|qq=}}, though it does not include bathing facilities. Downstream, approximately 2 km from the laundry area along the Yoshii River, there are also {{ill|Han'nya-ji Onsen|ja|般若寺温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Han'nya-ji Onsen|qq=}} and {{ill|Ōdzuri Onsen|ja|大釣温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdzuri Onsen|qq=}}; these are believed to be sourced from the same fault line as the upstream Okutsu Onsen, and in some contexts the entire area is collectively referred to as "Okutsu Onsen"日本温泉科学会（監修）『図説 日本の温泉 ―170温泉のサイエンス―』 p.133 朝倉書店 2020th year 3月1日発行 ISBN 978-4-254-16075-8. == History == According to the {{ill|Onsen Origin Myth|lt=Hot Spring Founding Legend|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|qq=}}, the hot spring was discovered by {{ill|Sukunabikona-no-mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo Period]], it served as a site for hot spring treatment ({{ill|Tōji|ja|湯治|1=Thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|1=Thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Thermal bath|qq=}}) for the [[:en:Tsuyama Domain|Tsuyama Domain]], and the area around the present-day foot‑stepping laundry is said to have once housed a "Locked Bath" ({{ill|Kagiyu|ja|鍵湯|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagiyu|qq=}}), from which usage was restricted by domain lord {{ill|Mori Tadamasu|ja|森忠政|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Tadamasu|qq=}}, who stationed guards to lock it. The cosmetics company [[:en:Kosé|KOSÉ]] has developed a lotion based on the components of this hot spring. Foot‑stepping laundry has long been practiced; its process was even commemorated in a tanka by [[:en:Tekkan Yosano|Yosano Tekkan]]:
衣洗う　奥津のおとめ　河床の　清きに立ちて　踊るごと践む
However, a large fire in 1926 destroyed the hot spring town山村 順次 『47都道府県・温泉百科』 p.213 丸善出版 2015th year 12月30日発行 ISBN 978-4-621-08996-5. The area is also known as the setting of [[:en:Akitsu Springs|Akitsu Onsen]], a novel by {{ill|Fujiwara Shini|ja|藤原審爾|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Shini|qq=}}, which was later adapted into a film in 1962 that boosted the prominence of Okutsu Onsen. On August 1, 1966, Okutsu Onsen was designated a {{ill|National Recreational Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Recreational Hot Spring Resort|qq=}}, and in 1991 it was designated as a {{ill|National Health Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保健温泉地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Health Hot Spring Resort|qq=}}. An express bus service, nicknamed the "Romance Car," operated by {{ill|Chūtetsu Bus|ja|中鉄バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūtetsu Bus|qq=}} connects [[:en:Okayama Station|Okayama Station]], [[:en:Tsuyama Station|Tsuyama Station]], and Okutsu Onsen (stopping in front of the roadside station on National Route 179) with one round trip per day. == Access == * '''Automobile''': Approximately 25 minutes by car from the [[:en:Chūgoku Expressway|Chūgoku Expressway]] and {{ill|Inshō Interchange|ja|院庄インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inshō Interchange|qq=}}. * '''Railway''': Alight at [[:en:Tsuyama Station|Tsuyama Station]] on the {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR West|qq=}} ([[:en:Kishin Line|Himeshin Line]], [[:en:Tsuyama Line|Tsuyama Line]]), then take the bus from bus terminal stop #5 operated by {{ill|Chūtetsu Hokubu Bus|ja|中鉄北部バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūtetsu Hokubu Bus|qq=}}: ** Board the Okutsu Onsen/Ishikoshi Line for about 50 minutes (operating 4 round trips per day; 2 round trips on weekends and holidays). ** Transfer to the {{ill|Gongo Bus|ja|ごんごバス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gongo Bus|qq=}} West Loop line, and at either "Marunaka Inshō Store" or "Plant 5," transfer to a community bus bound for the Kamisaihara Promotion Center (operating 4 round trips per day). * '''Highway Bus''': From {{ill|Namba|ja|なんば|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namba|qq=}} (via the {{ill|Osaka City Air Terminal|ja|なんば|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka City Air Terminal|qq=}}) and from [[:en:Shin-Ōsaka Station|Shin-Osaka Station]] (via the Hankyu High-Speed Bus Shin-Osaka Terminal at the North Exit), take the {{ill|Nikkō Kōsokubasu|ja|日交高速バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō Kōsokubasu|qq=}} on the line from [[:en:Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi City]] to [[:en:Kobe|Kobe City]]/Osaka. Alight at Okutsu Onsen-guchi (in front of the roadside station on [[:en:Japan National Route 179|National Route 179]]); there is one round trip per day. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{WikiPortal|Hot Springs}} * [[:en:List_of_hot_springs_in_Japan|List of Hot Spring Resorts in Japan]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Okutsu Onsen}} * [https://www.mto.ne.jp/okutsuonsenkk/ Okutsu Onsen Tourism Association] * [http://kanko.town.kagamino.lg.jp/hot_spring/okutsu-spa/ Kagamino Town Tourism Navi: Okutsu Onsen] * [https://afw-at.com/page.php?id=146 Okutsu Onsen] – At Town Web Magazine ''' ''' '''''' {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|奥津温泉|version=104384327|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot Springs in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreational Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Geography of Kagamino Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Legends]] [[Category:Onsen Origin Myth]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Un11]] }} [[sn:Okutsu Onsen]] [[ja:奥津温泉]] [[zh:温泉]] [[ja:嬉野温泉]] [[ja:武雄温泉]] [[ja:日本書紀]] [[ja:南紀白浜温泉]] [[ja:行基]] [[ja:空海]] [[ja:一遍]] [[ja:仏教]] [[ja:坂上田村麻呂]] [[ja:高野聖]] [[ja:大友頼泰]] [[ja:浜脇温泉]] [[ja:別府温泉]] [[ja:鉄輪温泉]] [[ja:火男火売神社]] [[ja:下呂温泉]] [[ja:山中温泉]] [[ja:道後温泉]] [[ja:城崎温泉]] [[ja:湯田温泉]] [[ja:三朝温泉]] [[ja:山鹿温泉]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] [[ja:老神温泉]] [[zh:温泉神社]] [[zh:温泉寺]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] [[en:Okutsu Onsen]] [[Category:国民保養温泉地]] [[Category:Hot springs of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:鏡野町の地理]]

{{infobox Onsen |Name = Okutsu Onsen |image = Okutsu Onsen 03.jpg |Comment = Day-trip Hot Spring Facility "Hanabijin no Sato" |Location = [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]] [[:en:Tomata District, Okayama|Tomata District]] [[:en:Kagamino|Kagamino Town]] {{Location map many | Japan Okayama Prefecture | relief = 1 | caption = | width = 220 | float = center | lat_deg = 35.2229 | lon_deg = 133.9201 | pos = top | label = '''Okutsu Onsen''' | lat2_deg = 35.0547 | lon2_deg = 134.0030 | mark2 = blue pog.svg | pos2 = right | label2 = [[:en:Tsuyama Station|Tsuyama Station]] | lat3_deg = 35.0624 | lon3_deg = 133.9495 | mark3 = Turquoise pog.svg | pos3 = left | label3 = {{ill|Inshō Interchange|ja|院庄I.C.|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inshō Interchange|qq=}} | lat4_deg = 34.6663 | lon4_deg = 133.9185 | mark4 = blue pog.svg | pos4 = right | label4 = [[:en:Okayama Station|Okayama Station]] }}
Map of Okayama Prefecture
|Latitude Degrees = 35 |Latitude Minutes = 13 |Latitude Seconds = 22.7 | N/S = N |Longitude Degrees = 133 |Longitude Minutes = 55 |Longitude Seconds = 12.6 | E/W = E |Access = Railway: [[:en:Kishin Line|Himeshin Line]], [[:en:Tsuyama Line|Tsuyama Line]] – from [[:en:Tsuyama Station|Tsuyama Station]], take {{ill|Chūtetsu Hokubu Bus|ja|中鉄北部バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūtetsu Hokubu Bus|qq=}} for about 50 minutes |Water Type = {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} |Temperature = 35 - 44 |Discharge = 1000 L (per minute) |pH = |Liquid Classification = {{ill|Alkaline|ja|アルカリ性|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alkaline|qq=}} |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Accommodations = |Total Capacity = |Annual Bath Visitors = |Statistical Year = |External links = [http://kanko.town.kagamino.lg.jp/hot_spring/okutsu-spa/ Kagamino Town Tourism Navi: Okutsu Onsen] |Special Notes = }} '''Okutsu Onsen''' (おくつ温泉) is a hot spring that emerges in [[:en:Kagamino|Kagamino Town]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. Located in the area formerly known as [[:en:Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka Province]], it is one of the "Mimasaka Three Hot Springs," together with [[:en:Yunogo_Onsen|Yunogo Onsen]] and {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|ceb|Yubara hotspring|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|qq=}}. == Water Quality == * {{ill|Alkaline Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alkaline Simple Hot Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} – Known as the "Hot Spring for Beauties"主婦の友社（編）『主婦の友ベストBooks 見直したい日本の「美」 日本の名湯100選』 p.134 主婦の友社 2010th year 3月10日発行 ISBN 978-4-07-270188-1. ** Source temperature: 35 - 44 ℃ ** Discharge: 1000 (L/min) == Onsen Town == [[File:Okutsu Onsen 02.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Footbath area]] A hot spring town is formed around the Okutsu Bridge spanning the [[:en:Yoshii River|Yoshii River]], with day-trip bathing facilities available. Along the riverbank, there are two {{ill|Outdoor Baths|ja|露天風呂|1=Rotenburo|1=Rotenburo|ko|노천탕|1=Rotenburo|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outdoor Baths|13=User:Immanuelle/Rotenburo|qq=}}; one of these is known as "Sentaku-yu" (Laundry Bath), where the practice of '''foot‑stepping laundry''' is famous. This method utilizes the drifting effect of the hot spring water—with a pH of 9.2—to aid in washing, a technique developed while keeping watch for bears and wolves in the surrounding mountains【湯の心旅】Okutsu Onsen (Okayama Prefecture) heralds spring with "foot‑stepping laundry" – washing away fatigue from within and without, as reported in the [[:en:The Nikkei|Nikkei]] morning edition, December 21, 2020 (separate printing, NIKKEI Plus 1, page 13)主婦の友社（編）『主婦の友ベストBooks 見直したい日本の「美」 日本の名湯100選』 p.135 主婦の友社 2010th year 3月10日発行 ISBN 978-4-07-270188-1. Today, some locals still use the baths for laundry, and live demonstrations for tourists are held on Sundays and public holidays from March to November. Along National Route 179, there is a roadside station, {{ill|Michinoeki Okutsu Onsen|ja|道の駅奥津温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoeki Okutsu Onsen|qq=}}, though it does not include bathing facilities. Downstream, approximately 2 km from the laundry area along the Yoshii River, there are also {{ill|Han'nya-ji Onsen|ja|般若寺温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Han'nya-ji Onsen|qq=}} and {{ill|Ōdzuri Onsen|ja|大釣温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdzuri Onsen|qq=}}; these are believed to be sourced from the same fault line as the upstream Okutsu Onsen, and in some contexts the entire area is collectively referred to as "Okutsu Onsen"日本温泉科学会（監修）『図説 日本の温泉 ―170温泉のサイエンス―』 p.133 朝倉書店 2020th year 3月1日発行 ISBN 978-4-254-16075-8. == History == According to the {{ill|Onsen Origin Myth|lt=Hot Spring Founding Legend|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|qq=}}, the hot spring was discovered by {{ill|Sukunabikona-no-mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo Period]], it served as a site for hot spring treatment ({{ill|Tōji|ja|湯治|1=Thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|1=Thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Thermal bath|qq=}}) for the [[:en:Tsuyama Domain|Tsuyama Domain]], and the area around the present-day foot‑stepping laundry is said to have once housed a "Locked Bath" ({{ill|Kagiyu|ja|鍵湯|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagiyu|qq=}}), from which usage was restricted by domain lord {{ill|Mori Tadamasu|ja|森忠政|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Tadamasu|qq=}}, who stationed guards to lock it. The cosmetics company [[:en:Kosé|KOSÉ]] has developed a lotion based on the components of this hot spring. Foot‑stepping laundry has long been practiced; its process was even commemorated in a tanka by [[:en:Tekkan Yosano|Yosano Tekkan]]:
衣洗う　奥津のおとめ　河床の　清きに立ちて　踊るごと践む
However, a large fire in 1926 destroyed the hot spring town山村 順次 『47都道府県・温泉百科』 p.213 丸善出版 2015th year 12月30日発行 ISBN 978-4-621-08996-5. The area is also known as the setting of [[:en:Akitsu Springs|Akitsu Onsen]], a novel by {{ill|Fujiwara Shini|ja|藤原審爾|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Shini|qq=}}, which was later adapted into a film in 1962 that boosted the prominence of Okutsu Onsen. On August 1, 1966, Okutsu Onsen was designated a {{ill|National Recreational Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Recreational Hot Spring Resort|qq=}}, and in 1991 it was designated as a {{ill|National Health Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保健温泉地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Health Hot Spring Resort|qq=}}. An express bus service, nicknamed the "Romance Car," operated by {{ill|Chūtetsu Bus|ja|中鉄バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūtetsu Bus|qq=}} connects [[:en:Okayama Station|Okayama Station]], [[:en:Tsuyama Station|Tsuyama Station]], and Okutsu Onsen (stopping in front of the roadside station on National Route 179) with one round trip per day. == Access == * '''Automobile''': Approximately 25 minutes by car from the [[:en:Chūgoku Expressway|Chūgoku Expressway]] and {{ill|Inshō Interchange|ja|院庄インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inshō Interchange|qq=}}. * '''Railway''': Alight at [[:en:Tsuyama Station|Tsuyama Station]] on the {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR West|qq=}} ([[:en:Kishin Line|Himeshin Line]], [[:en:Tsuyama Line|Tsuyama Line]]), then take the bus from bus terminal stop #5 operated by {{ill|Chūtetsu Hokubu Bus|ja|中鉄北部バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūtetsu Hokubu Bus|qq=}}: ** Board the Okutsu Onsen/Ishikoshi Line for about 50 minutes (operating 4 round trips per day; 2 round trips on weekends and holidays). ** Transfer to the {{ill|Gongo Bus|ja|ごんごバス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gongo Bus|qq=}} West Loop line, and at either "Marunaka Inshō Store" or "Plant 5," transfer to a community bus bound for the Kamisaihara Promotion Center (operating 4 round trips per day). * '''Highway Bus''': From {{ill|Namba|ja|なんば|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namba|qq=}} (via the {{ill|Osaka City Air Terminal|ja|なんば|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka City Air Terminal|qq=}}) and from [[:en:Shin-Ōsaka Station|Shin-Osaka Station]] (via the Hankyu High-Speed Bus Shin-Osaka Terminal at the North Exit), take the {{ill|Nikkō Kōsokubasu|ja|日交高速バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō Kōsokubasu|qq=}} on the line from [[:en:Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi City]] to [[:en:Kobe|Kobe City]]/Osaka. Alight at Okutsu Onsen-guchi (in front of the roadside station on [[:en:Japan National Route 179|National Route 179]]); there is one round trip per day. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{WikiPortal|Hot Springs}} * [[:en:List_of_hot_springs_in_Japan|List of Hot Spring Resorts in Japan]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Okutsu Onsen}} * [https://www.mto.ne.jp/okutsuonsenkk/ Okutsu Onsen Tourism Association] * [http://kanko.town.kagamino.lg.jp/hot_spring/okutsu-spa/ Kagamino Town Tourism Navi: Okutsu Onsen] * [https://afw-at.com/page.php?id=146 Okutsu Onsen] – At Town Web Magazine ''' ''' '''''' {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|奥津温泉|version=104384327|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot Springs in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreational Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Geography of Kagamino Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Legends]] [[Category:Onsen Origin Myth]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Un11]] }} [[sn:Okutsu Onsen]] [[ja:奥津温泉]] [[zh:温泉]] [[ja:嬉野温泉]] [[ja:武雄温泉]] [[ja:日本書紀]] [[ja:南紀白浜温泉]] [[ja:行基]] [[ja:空海]] [[ja:一遍]] [[ja:仏教]] [[ja:坂上田村麻呂]] [[ja:高野聖]] [[ja:大友頼泰]] [[ja:浜脇温泉]] [[ja:別府温泉]] [[ja:鉄輪温泉]] [[ja:火男火売神社]] [[ja:下呂温泉]] [[ja:山中温泉]] [[ja:道後温泉]] [[ja:城崎温泉]] [[ja:湯田温泉]] [[ja:三朝温泉]] [[ja:山鹿温泉]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] [[ja:老神温泉]] [[zh:温泉神社]] [[zh:温泉寺]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] [[Category:国民保養温泉地]] [[Category:Hot springs of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:鏡野町の地理]]
