[[co:commons:Category:Okutsu Onsen]][[ja:奥津温泉]] [[co:commons:Category:Okutsu Onsen]][[ja:奥津温泉]] [[sn:commons:Category:Okutsu Onsen]][[ja:奥津温泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11445969]] Wikidata name: Okutsu Onsen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Onsen |Name = Okutsu Onsen |image = Okutsu Onsen 03.jpg |Comment = Day-trip Hot Spring Facility "Hanabijin no Sato" |Location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}} {{ill|Tomata District, Okayama|en|Tomata District, Okayama|WD=Q1131549|lt=Tomata District}} {{ill|Kagamino|en|Kagamino|WD=Q1155346|lt=Kagamino Town}} {{Location map many | Japan Okayama Prefecture | relief = 1 | caption = | width = 220 | float = center | lat_deg = 35.2229 | lon_deg = 133.9201 | pos = top | label = '''Okutsu Onsen''' | lat2_deg = 35.0547 | lon2_deg = 134.0030 | mark2 = blue pog.svg | pos2 = right | label2 = {{ill|Tsuyama Station|en|Tsuyama Station|WD=Q844686|lt=Tsuyama Station}} | lat3_deg = 35.0624 | lon3_deg = 133.9495 | mark3 = Turquoise pog.svg | pos3 = left | label3 = {{ill|Inshō Interchange|ja|院庄I.C.|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inshō Interchange|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | lat4_deg = 34.6663 | lon4_deg = 133.9185 | mark4 = blue pog.svg | pos4 = right | label4 = {{ill|Okayama Station|en|Okayama Station|WD=Q801293|lt=Okayama Station}} }}
Map of Okayama Prefecture
|Latitude Degrees = 35 |Latitude Minutes = 13 |Latitude Seconds = 22.7 | N/S = N |Longitude Degrees = 133 |Longitude Minutes = 55 |Longitude Seconds = 12.6 | E/W = E |Access = Railway: {{ill|Kishin Line|en|Kishin Line|WD=Q869221|lt=Himeshin Line}}, {{ill|Tsuyama Line|en|Tsuyama Line|WD=Q7850362|lt=Tsuyama Line}} – from {{ill|Tsuyama Station|en|Tsuyama Station|WD=Q844686|lt=Tsuyama Station}}, take {{ill|Chūtetsu Hokubu Bus|ja|中鉄北部バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūtetsu Hokubu Bus|1=Chūtetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11367773}} for about 50 minutes |Water Type = {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} |Temperature = 35 - 44 |Discharge = 1000 L (per minute) |pH = |Liquid Classification = {{ill|Alkaline|ja|アルカリ性|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alkaline|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 塩基|1=base|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11193}} |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Accommodations = |Total Capacity = |Annual Bath Visitors = |Statistical Year = |External links = [http://kanko.town.kagamino.lg.jp/hot_spring/okutsu-spa/ Kagamino Town Tourism Navi: Okutsu Onsen] |Special Notes = }} '''Okutsu Onsen''' (おくつ温泉) is a hot spring that emerges in {{ill|Kagamino|en|Kagamino|WD=Q1155346|lt=Kagamino Town}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}. Located in the area formerly known as {{ill|Mimasaka Province|en|Mimasaka Province|WD=Q936036|lt=Mimasaka Province}}, it is one of the "Mimasaka Three Hot Springs," together with {{ill|Yunogo_Onsen|en|Yunogo_Onsen|WD=Q8061351|lt=Yunogo Onsen}} and {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|ceb|Yubara hotspring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563486}}. == Water Quality == * {{ill|Alkaline Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alkaline Simple Hot Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} – Known as the "Hot Spring for Beauties"主婦の友社（編）『主婦の友ベストBooks 見直したい日本の「美」 日本の名湯100選』 p.134 主婦の友社 2010th year 3月10日発行 ISBN 978-4-07-270188-1. ** Source temperature: 35 - 44 ℃ ** Discharge: 1000 (L/min) == Onsen Town == [[File:Okutsu Onsen 02.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Footbath area]] A hot spring town is formed around the Okutsu Bridge spanning the {{ill|Yoshii River|en|Yoshii River|WD=Q11412583|lt=Yoshii River}}, with day-trip bathing facilities available. Along the riverbank, there are two {{ill|Outdoor Baths|ja|露天風呂|1=Outdoor hot spring baths|1=Outdoor hot spring baths|ko|노천탕|1=Outdoor hot spring baths|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outdoor Baths|13=User:Immanuelle/Outdoor hot spring baths|qq=}}; one of these is known as "Sentaku-yu" (Laundry Bath), where the practice of '''foot‑stepping laundry''' is famous. This method utilizes the drifting effect of the hot spring water—with a pH of 9.2—to aid in washing, a technique developed while keeping watch for bears and wolves in the surrounding mountains【湯の心旅】Okutsu Onsen (Okayama Prefecture) heralds spring with "foot‑stepping laundry" – washing away fatigue from within and without, as reported in the {{ill|The Nikkei|en|The Nikkei|WD=Q1077694|lt=Nikkei}} morning edition, December 21, 2020 (separate printing, NIKKEI Plus 1, page 13)主婦の友社（編）『主婦の友ベストBooks 見直したい日本の「美」 日本の名湯100選』 p.135 主婦の友社 2010th year 3月10日発行 ISBN 978-4-07-270188-1. Today, some locals still use the baths for laundry, and live demonstrations for tourists are held on Sundays and public holidays from March to November. Along National Route 179, there is a roadside station, {{ill|Michinoeki Okutsu Onsen|ja|道の駅奥津温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoeki Okutsu Onsen|1=Roadside station Okutsu-onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641244}}, though it does not include bathing facilities. Downstream, approximately 2 km from the laundry area along the Yoshii River, there are also {{ill|Han'nya-ji Onsen|ja|般若寺温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Han'nya-ji Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Ōdzuri Onsen|ja|大釣温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdzuri Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}; these are believed to be sourced from the same fault line as the upstream Okutsu Onsen, and in some contexts the entire area is collectively referred to as "Okutsu Onsen"日本温泉科学会（監修）『図説 日本の温泉 ―170温泉のサイエンス―』 p.133 朝倉書店 2020th year 3月1日発行 ISBN 978-4-254-16075-8. == History == According to the {{ill|Onsen Origin Myth|lt=Hot Spring Founding Legend|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|qq=}}, the hot spring was discovered by {{ill|Sukunabikona-no-mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo Period}}, it served as a site for hot spring treatment ({{ill|Tōji|ja|湯治|1=thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|1=Thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Thermal bath|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1004887}}) for the {{ill|Tsuyama Domain|en|Tsuyama Domain|WD=Q1058224|lt=Tsuyama Domain}}, and the area around the present-day foot‑stepping laundry is said to have once housed a "Locked Bath" ({{ill|Kagiyu|ja|鍵湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagiyu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), from which usage was restricted by domain lord {{ill|Mori Tadamasu|ja|森忠政|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Tadamasu|1=Mori Tadamasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11117105}}, who stationed guards to lock it. The cosmetics company {{ill|Kosé|en|Kosé|WD=Q18857532|lt=KOSÉ}} has developed a lotion based on the components of this hot spring. Foot‑stepping laundry has long been practiced; its process was even commemorated in a tanka by {{ill|Tekkan Yosano|en|Tekkan Yosano|WD=Q1206405|lt=Yosano Tekkan}}:
衣洗う　奥津のおとめ　河床の　清きに立ちて　踊るごと践む
However, a large fire in 1926 destroyed the hot spring town山村 順次 『47都道府県・温泉百科』 p.213 丸善出版 2015th year 12月30日発行 ISBN 978-4-621-08996-5. The area is also known as the setting of {{ill|Akitsu Springs|en|Akitsu Springs|WD=Q3212936|lt=Akitsu Onsen}}, a novel by {{ill|Fujiwara Shini|ja|藤原審爾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Shini|1=Shinji Fujiwara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623205}}, which was later adapted into a film in 1962 that boosted the prominence of Okutsu Onsen. On August 1, 1966, Okutsu Onsen was designated a {{ill|National Recreational Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Recreational Hot Spring Resort|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}}, and in 1991 it was designated as a {{ill|National Health Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保健温泉地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Health Hot Spring Resort|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国民保養温泉地|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}}. An express bus service, nicknamed the "Romance Car," operated by {{ill|Chūtetsu Bus|ja|中鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūtetsu Bus|1=Chutetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11367772}} connects {{ill|Okayama Station|en|Okayama Station|WD=Q801293|lt=Okayama Station}}, {{ill|Tsuyama Station|en|Tsuyama Station|WD=Q844686|lt=Tsuyama Station}}, and Okutsu Onsen (stopping in front of the roadside station on National Route 179) with one round trip per day. == Access == * '''Automobile''': Approximately 25 minutes by car from the {{ill|Chūgoku Expressway|en|Chūgoku Expressway|WD=Q1091207|lt=Chūgoku Expressway}} and {{ill|Inshō Interchange|ja|院庄インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inshō Interchange|1=Innosho interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658129}}. * '''Railway''': Alight at {{ill|Tsuyama Station|en|Tsuyama Station|WD=Q844686|lt=Tsuyama Station}} on the {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR West|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西日本旅客鉄道|1=West Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q502125}} ({{ill|Kishin Line|en|Kishin Line|WD=Q869221|lt=Himeshin Line}}, {{ill|Tsuyama Line|en|Tsuyama Line|WD=Q7850362|lt=Tsuyama Line}}), then take the bus from bus terminal stop #5 operated by {{ill|Chūtetsu Hokubu Bus|ja|中鉄北部バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūtetsu Hokubu Bus|1=Chūtetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11367773}}: ** Board the Okutsu Onsen/Ishikoshi Line for about 50 minutes (operating 4 round trips per day; 2 round trips on weekends and holidays). ** Transfer to the {{ill|Gongo Bus|ja|ごんごバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gongo Bus|1=Gongo Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20044406}} West Loop line, and at either "Marunaka Inshō Store" or "Plant 5," transfer to a community bus bound for the Kamisaihara Promotion Center (operating 4 round trips per day). * '''Highway Bus''': From {{ill|Namba|ja|なんば|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namba|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ナンバ|1=Namba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1503790}} (via the {{ill|Osaka City Air Terminal|ja|なんば|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka City Air Terminal|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ナンバ|1=Namba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1503790}}) and from {{ill|Shin-Ōsaka Station|en|Shin-Ōsaka Station|WD=Q801438|lt=Shin-Osaka Station}} (via the Hankyu High-Speed Bus Shin-Osaka Terminal at the North Exit), take the {{ill|Nikkō Kōsokubasu|ja|日交高速バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō Kōsokubasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the line from {{ill|Kurayoshi, Tottori|en|Kurayoshi, Tottori|WD=Q642006|lt=Kurayoshi City}} to {{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|WD=Q48320|lt=Kobe City}}/Osaka. Alight at Okutsu Onsen-guchi (in front of the roadside station on {{ill|Japan National Route 179|en|Japan National Route 179|WD=Q6156867|lt=National Route 179}}); there is one round trip per day. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{WikiPortal|Hot Springs}} * {{ill|List_of_hot_springs_in_Japan|en|List_of_hot_springs_in_Japan|WD=Q11086467|lt=List of Hot Spring Resorts in Japan}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Okutsu Onsen}} * [https://www.mto.ne.jp/okutsuonsenkk/ Okutsu Onsen Tourism Association] * [http://kanko.town.kagamino.lg.jp/hot_spring/okutsu-spa/ Kagamino Town Tourism Navi: Okutsu Onsen] * [https://afw-at.com/page.php?id=146 Okutsu Onsen] – At Town Web Magazine ''' ''' '''''' {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|奥津温泉|version=104384327|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:奥津温泉]] [[zh:温泉]] [[ja:嬉野温泉]] [[ja:武雄温泉]] [[ja:日本書紀]] [[ja:南紀白浜温泉]] [[ja:行基]] [[ja:空海]] [[ja:一遍]] [[ja:仏教]] [[ja:坂上田村麻呂]] [[ja:高野聖]] [[ja:大友頼泰]] [[ja:浜脇温泉]] [[ja:別府温泉]] [[ja:鉄輪温泉]] [[ja:火男火売神社]] [[ja:下呂温泉]] [[ja:山中温泉]] [[ja:道後温泉]] [[ja:城崎温泉]] [[ja:湯田温泉]] [[ja:三朝温泉]] [[ja:山鹿温泉]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] [[ja:老神温泉]] [[zh:温泉神社]] [[zh:温泉寺]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] {{Commons category|Okutsu Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11445969}} [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Geography of Kagamino Town]] [[Category:Hot springs of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Legends]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Okutsu Onsen]][[ja:奥津温泉]] [[co:commons:Category:Okutsu Onsen]][[ja:奥津温泉]] [[sn:commons:Category:Okutsu Onsen]][[ja:奥津温泉]] {{infobox Onsen |Name = Okutsu Onsen |image = Okutsu Onsen 03.jpg |Comment = Day-trip Hot Spring Facility "Hanabijin no Sato" |Location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}} {{ill|Tomata District, Okayama|en|Tomata District, Okayama|WD=Q1131549|lt=Tomata District}} {{ill|Kagamino|en|Kagamino|WD=Q1155346|lt=Kagamino Town}} {{Location map many | Japan Okayama Prefecture | relief = 1 | caption = | width = 220 | float = center | lat_deg = 35.2229 | lon_deg = 133.9201 | pos = top | label = '''Okutsu Onsen''' | lat2_deg = 35.0547 | lon2_deg = 134.0030 | mark2 = blue pog.svg | pos2 = right | label2 = {{ill|Tsuyama Station|en|Tsuyama Station|WD=Q844686|lt=Tsuyama Station}} | lat3_deg = 35.0624 | lon3_deg = 133.9495 | mark3 = Turquoise pog.svg | pos3 = left | label3 = {{ill|Inshō Interchange|ja|院庄I.C.|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inshō Interchange|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | lat4_deg = 34.6663 | lon4_deg = 133.9185 | mark4 = blue pog.svg | pos4 = right | label4 = {{ill|Okayama Station|en|Okayama Station|WD=Q801293|lt=Okayama Station}} }}
Map of Okayama Prefecture
|Latitude Degrees = 35 |Latitude Minutes = 13 |Latitude Seconds = 22.7 | N/S = N |Longitude Degrees = 133 |Longitude Minutes = 55 |Longitude Seconds = 12.6 | E/W = E |Access = Railway: {{ill|Kishin Line|en|Kishin Line|WD=Q869221|lt=Himeshin Line}}, {{ill|Tsuyama Line|en|Tsuyama Line|WD=Q7850362|lt=Tsuyama Line}} – from {{ill|Tsuyama Station|en|Tsuyama Station|WD=Q844686|lt=Tsuyama Station}}, take {{ill|Chūtetsu Hokubu Bus|ja|中鉄北部バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūtetsu Hokubu Bus|1=Chūtetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11367773}} for about 50 minutes |Water Type = {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} |Temperature = 35 - 44 |Discharge = 1000 L (per minute) |pH = |Liquid Classification = {{ill|Alkaline|ja|アルカリ性|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alkaline|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 塩基|1=base|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11193}} |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Accommodations = |Total Capacity = |Annual Bath Visitors = |Statistical Year = |External links = [http://kanko.town.kagamino.lg.jp/hot_spring/okutsu-spa/ Kagamino Town Tourism Navi: Okutsu Onsen] |Special Notes = }} '''Okutsu Onsen''' (おくつ温泉) is a hot spring that emerges in {{ill|Kagamino|en|Kagamino|WD=Q1155346|lt=Kagamino Town}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}. Located in the area formerly known as {{ill|Mimasaka Province|en|Mimasaka Province|WD=Q936036|lt=Mimasaka Province}}, it is one of the "Mimasaka Three Hot Springs," together with {{ill|Yunogo_Onsen|en|Yunogo_Onsen|WD=Q8061351|lt=Yunogo Onsen}} and {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|ceb|Yubara hotspring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563486}}. == Water Quality == * {{ill|Alkaline Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alkaline Simple Hot Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} – Known as the "Hot Spring for Beauties"主婦の友社（編）『主婦の友ベストBooks 見直したい日本の「美」 日本の名湯100選』 p.134 主婦の友社 2010th year 3月10日発行 ISBN 978-4-07-270188-1. ** Source temperature: 35 - 44 ℃ ** Discharge: 1000 (L/min) == Onsen Town == [[File:Okutsu Onsen 02.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Footbath area]] A hot spring town is formed around the Okutsu Bridge spanning the {{ill|Yoshii River|en|Yoshii River|WD=Q11412583|lt=Yoshii River}}, with day-trip bathing facilities available. Along the riverbank, there are two {{ill|Outdoor Baths|ja|露天風呂|1=Outdoor hot spring baths|1=Outdoor hot spring baths|ko|노천탕|1=Outdoor hot spring baths|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outdoor Baths|13=User:Immanuelle/Outdoor hot spring baths|qq=}}; one of these is known as "Sentaku-yu" (Laundry Bath), where the practice of '''foot‑stepping laundry''' is famous. This method utilizes the drifting effect of the hot spring water—with a pH of 9.2—to aid in washing, a technique developed while keeping watch for bears and wolves in the surrounding mountains【湯の心旅】Okutsu Onsen (Okayama Prefecture) heralds spring with "foot‑stepping laundry" – washing away fatigue from within and without, as reported in the {{ill|The Nikkei|en|The Nikkei|WD=Q1077694|lt=Nikkei}} morning edition, December 21, 2020 (separate printing, NIKKEI Plus 1, page 13)主婦の友社（編）『主婦の友ベストBooks 見直したい日本の「美」 日本の名湯100選』 p.135 主婦の友社 2010th year 3月10日発行 ISBN 978-4-07-270188-1. Today, some locals still use the baths for laundry, and live demonstrations for tourists are held on Sundays and public holidays from March to November. Along National Route 179, there is a roadside station, {{ill|Michinoeki Okutsu Onsen|ja|道の駅奥津温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoeki Okutsu Onsen|1=Roadside station Okutsu-onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641244}}, though it does not include bathing facilities. Downstream, approximately 2 km from the laundry area along the Yoshii River, there are also {{ill|Han'nya-ji Onsen|ja|般若寺温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Han'nya-ji Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Ōdzuri Onsen|ja|大釣温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdzuri Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}; these are believed to be sourced from the same fault line as the upstream Okutsu Onsen, and in some contexts the entire area is collectively referred to as "Okutsu Onsen"日本温泉科学会（監修）『図説 日本の温泉 ―170温泉のサイエンス―』 p.133 朝倉書店 2020th year 3月1日発行 ISBN 978-4-254-16075-8. == History == According to the {{ill|Onsen Origin Myth|lt=Hot Spring Founding Legend|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|qq=}}, the hot spring was discovered by {{ill|Sukunabikona-no-mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo Period}}, it served as a site for hot spring treatment ({{ill|Tōji|ja|湯治|1=thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|1=Thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Thermal bath|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1004887}}) for the {{ill|Tsuyama Domain|en|Tsuyama Domain|WD=Q1058224|lt=Tsuyama Domain}}, and the area around the present-day foot‑stepping laundry is said to have once housed a "Locked Bath" ({{ill|Kagiyu|ja|鍵湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagiyu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), from which usage was restricted by domain lord {{ill|Mori Tadamasu|ja|森忠政|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Tadamasu|1=Mori Tadamasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11117105}}, who stationed guards to lock it. The cosmetics company {{ill|Kosé|en|Kosé|WD=Q18857532|lt=KOSÉ}} has developed a lotion based on the components of this hot spring. Foot‑stepping laundry has long been practiced; its process was even commemorated in a tanka by {{ill|Tekkan Yosano|en|Tekkan Yosano|WD=Q1206405|lt=Yosano Tekkan}}:
衣洗う　奥津のおとめ　河床の　清きに立ちて　踊るごと践む
However, a large fire in 1926 destroyed the hot spring town山村 順次 『47都道府県・温泉百科』 p.213 丸善出版 2015th year 12月30日発行 ISBN 978-4-621-08996-5. The area is also known as the setting of {{ill|Akitsu Springs|en|Akitsu Springs|WD=Q3212936|lt=Akitsu Onsen}}, a novel by {{ill|Fujiwara Shini|ja|藤原審爾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Shini|1=Shinji Fujiwara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623205}}, which was later adapted into a film in 1962 that boosted the prominence of Okutsu Onsen. On August 1, 1966, Okutsu Onsen was designated a {{ill|National Recreational Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Recreational Hot Spring Resort|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}}, and in 1991 it was designated as a {{ill|National Health Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保健温泉地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Health Hot Spring Resort|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国民保養温泉地|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}}. An express bus service, nicknamed the "Romance Car," operated by {{ill|Chūtetsu Bus|ja|中鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūtetsu Bus|1=Chutetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11367772}} connects {{ill|Okayama Station|en|Okayama Station|WD=Q801293|lt=Okayama Station}}, {{ill|Tsuyama Station|en|Tsuyama Station|WD=Q844686|lt=Tsuyama Station}}, and Okutsu Onsen (stopping in front of the roadside station on National Route 179) with one round trip per day. == Access == * '''Automobile''': Approximately 25 minutes by car from the {{ill|Chūgoku Expressway|en|Chūgoku Expressway|WD=Q1091207|lt=Chūgoku Expressway}} and {{ill|Inshō Interchange|ja|院庄インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inshō Interchange|1=Innosho interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658129}}. * '''Railway''': Alight at {{ill|Tsuyama Station|en|Tsuyama Station|WD=Q844686|lt=Tsuyama Station}} on the {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR West|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西日本旅客鉄道|1=West Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q502125}} ({{ill|Kishin Line|en|Kishin Line|WD=Q869221|lt=Himeshin Line}}, {{ill|Tsuyama Line|en|Tsuyama Line|WD=Q7850362|lt=Tsuyama Line}}), then take the bus from bus terminal stop #5 operated by {{ill|Chūtetsu Hokubu Bus|ja|中鉄北部バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūtetsu Hokubu Bus|1=Chūtetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11367773}}: ** Board the Okutsu Onsen/Ishikoshi Line for about 50 minutes (operating 4 round trips per day; 2 round trips on weekends and holidays). ** Transfer to the {{ill|Gongo Bus|ja|ごんごバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gongo Bus|1=Gongo Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20044406}} West Loop line, and at either "Marunaka Inshō Store" or "Plant 5," transfer to a community bus bound for the Kamisaihara Promotion Center (operating 4 round trips per day). * '''Highway Bus''': From {{ill|Namba|ja|なんば|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namba|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ナンバ|1=Namba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1503790}} (via the {{ill|Osaka City Air Terminal|ja|なんば|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka City Air Terminal|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ナンバ|1=Namba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1503790}}) and from {{ill|Shin-Ōsaka Station|en|Shin-Ōsaka Station|WD=Q801438|lt=Shin-Osaka Station}} (via the Hankyu High-Speed Bus Shin-Osaka Terminal at the North Exit), take the {{ill|Nikkō Kōsokubasu|ja|日交高速バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō Kōsokubasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the line from {{ill|Kurayoshi, Tottori|en|Kurayoshi, Tottori|WD=Q642006|lt=Kurayoshi City}} to {{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|WD=Q48320|lt=Kobe City}}/Osaka. Alight at Okutsu Onsen-guchi (in front of the roadside station on {{ill|Japan National Route 179|en|Japan National Route 179|WD=Q6156867|lt=National Route 179}}); there is one round trip per day. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{WikiPortal|Hot Springs}} * {{ill|List_of_hot_springs_in_Japan|en|List_of_hot_springs_in_Japan|WD=Q11086467|lt=List of Hot Spring Resorts in Japan}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Okutsu Onsen}} * [https://www.mto.ne.jp/okutsuonsenkk/ Okutsu Onsen Tourism Association] * [http://kanko.town.kagamino.lg.jp/hot_spring/okutsu-spa/ Kagamino Town Tourism Navi: Okutsu Onsen] * [https://afw-at.com/page.php?id=146 Okutsu Onsen] – At Town Web Magazine ''' ''' '''''' {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|奥津温泉|version=104384327|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:奥津温泉]] [[zh:温泉]] [[ja:嬉野温泉]] [[ja:武雄温泉]] [[ja:日本書紀]] [[ja:南紀白浜温泉]] [[ja:行基]] [[ja:空海]] [[ja:一遍]] [[ja:仏教]] [[ja:坂上田村麻呂]] [[ja:高野聖]] [[ja:大友頼泰]] [[ja:浜脇温泉]] [[ja:別府温泉]] [[ja:鉄輪温泉]] [[ja:火男火売神社]] [[ja:下呂温泉]] [[ja:山中温泉]] [[ja:道後温泉]] [[ja:城崎温泉]] [[ja:湯田温泉]] [[ja:三朝温泉]] [[ja:山鹿温泉]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] [[ja:老神温泉]] [[zh:温泉神社]] [[zh:温泉寺]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] {{Commons category|Okutsu Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11445969}} [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Geography of Kagamino Town]] [[Category:Hot springs of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Legends]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

|Latitude Degrees = 35 |Latitude Minutes = 13 |Latitude Seconds = 22.7 | N/S = N |Longitude Degrees = 133 |Longitude Minutes = 55 |Longitude Seconds = 12.6 | E/W = E |Access = Railway: {{ill|Kishin Line|en|Kishin Line|WD=Q869221|lt=Himeshin Line}}, {{ill|Tsuyama Line|en|Tsuyama Line|WD=Q7850362|lt=Tsuyama Line}} – from {{ill|Tsuyama Station|en|Tsuyama Station|WD=Q844686|lt=Tsuyama Station}}, take {{ill|Chūtetsu Hokubu Bus|ja|中鉄北部バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūtetsu Hokubu Bus|1=Chūtetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11367773}} for about 50 minutes |Water Type = {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} |Temperature = 35 - 44 |Discharge = 1000 L (per minute) |pH = |Liquid Classification = {{ill|Alkaline|ja|アルカリ性|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alkaline|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 塩基|1=base|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11193}} |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Accommodations = |Total Capacity = |Annual Bath Visitors = |Statistical Year = |External links = [http://kanko.town.kagamino.lg.jp/hot_spring/okutsu-spa/ Kagamino Town Tourism Navi: Okutsu Onsen] |Special Notes = }} '''Okutsu Onsen''' (おくつ温泉) is a hot spring that emerges in {{ill|Kagamino|en|Kagamino|WD=Q1155346|lt=Kagamino Town}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}. Located in the area formerly known as {{ill|Mimasaka Province|en|Mimasaka Province|WD=Q936036|lt=Mimasaka Province}}, it is one of the "Mimasaka Three Hot Springs," together with {{ill|Yunogo_Onsen|en|Yunogo_Onsen|WD=Q8061351|lt=Yunogo Onsen}} and {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|ceb|Yubara hotspring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563486}}. == Water Quality == * {{ill|Alkaline Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alkaline Simple Hot Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} – Known as the "Hot Spring for Beauties"主婦の友社（編）『主婦の友ベストBooks 見直したい日本の「美」 日本の名湯100選』 p.134 主婦の友社 2010th year 3月10日発行 ISBN 978-4-07-270188-1. ** Source temperature: 35 - 44 ℃ ** Discharge: 1000 (L/min) == Onsen Town == [[File:Okutsu Onsen 02.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Footbath area]] A hot spring town is formed around the Okutsu Bridge spanning the {{ill|Yoshii River|en|Yoshii River|WD=Q11412583|lt=Yoshii River}}, with day-trip bathing facilities available. Along the riverbank, there are two {{ill|Outdoor Baths|ja|露天風呂|1=Outdoor hot spring baths|1=Outdoor hot spring baths|ko|노천탕|1=Outdoor hot spring baths|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outdoor Baths|13=User:Immanuelle/Outdoor hot spring baths|qq=}}; one of these is known as "Sentaku-yu" (Laundry Bath), where the practice of '''foot‑stepping laundry''' is famous. This method utilizes the drifting effect of the hot spring water—with a pH of 9.2—to aid in washing, a technique developed while keeping watch for bears and wolves in the surrounding mountains【湯の心旅】Okutsu Onsen (Okayama Prefecture) heralds spring with "foot‑stepping laundry" – washing away fatigue from within and without, as reported in the {{ill|The Nikkei|en|The Nikkei|WD=Q1077694|lt=Nikkei}} morning edition, December 21, 2020 (separate printing, NIKKEI Plus 1, page 13)主婦の友社（編）『主婦の友ベストBooks 見直したい日本の「美」 日本の名湯100選』 p.135 主婦の友社 2010th year 3月10日発行 ISBN 978-4-07-270188-1. Today, some locals still use the baths for laundry, and live demonstrations for tourists are held on Sundays and public holidays from March to November. Along National Route 179, there is a roadside station, {{ill|Michinoeki Okutsu Onsen|ja|道の駅奥津温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoeki Okutsu Onsen|1=Roadside station Okutsu-onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641244}}, though it does not include bathing facilities. Downstream, approximately 2 km from the laundry area along the Yoshii River, there are also {{ill|Han'nya-ji Onsen|ja|般若寺温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Han'nya-ji Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Ōdzuri Onsen|ja|大釣温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdzuri Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}; these are believed to be sourced from the same fault line as the upstream Okutsu Onsen, and in some contexts the entire area is collectively referred to as "Okutsu Onsen"日本温泉科学会（監修）『図説 日本の温泉 ―170温泉のサイエンス―』 p.133 朝倉書店 2020th year 3月1日発行 ISBN 978-4-254-16075-8. == History == According to the {{ill|Onsen Origin Myth|lt=Hot Spring Founding Legend|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|qq=}}, the hot spring was discovered by {{ill|Sukunabikona-no-mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo Period}}, it served as a site for hot spring treatment ({{ill|Tōji|ja|湯治|1=thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|1=Thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Thermal bath|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1004887}}) for the {{ill|Tsuyama Domain|en|Tsuyama Domain|WD=Q1058224|lt=Tsuyama Domain}}, and the area around the present-day foot‑stepping laundry is said to have once housed a "Locked Bath" ({{ill|Kagiyu|ja|鍵湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagiyu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), from which usage was restricted by domain lord {{ill|Mori Tadamasu|ja|森忠政|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Tadamasu|1=Mori Tadamasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11117105}}, who stationed guards to lock it. The cosmetics company {{ill|Kosé|en|Kosé|WD=Q18857532|lt=KOSÉ}} has developed a lotion based on the components of this hot spring. Foot‑stepping laundry has long been practiced; its process was even commemorated in a tanka by {{ill|Tekkan Yosano|en|Tekkan Yosano|WD=Q1206405|lt=Yosano Tekkan}}:
衣洗う　奥津のおとめ　河床の　清きに立ちて　踊るごと践む
However, a large fire in 1926 destroyed the hot spring town山村 順次 『47都道府県・温泉百科』 p.213 丸善出版 2015th year 12月30日発行 ISBN 978-4-621-08996-5. The area is also known as the setting of {{ill|Akitsu Springs|en|Akitsu Springs|WD=Q3212936|lt=Akitsu Onsen}}, a novel by {{ill|Fujiwara Shini|ja|藤原審爾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Shini|1=Shinji Fujiwara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623205}}, which was later adapted into a film in 1962 that boosted the prominence of Okutsu Onsen. On August 1, 1966, Okutsu Onsen was designated a {{ill|National Recreational Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Recreational Hot Spring Resort|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}}, and in 1991 it was designated as a {{ill|National Health Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保健温泉地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Health Hot Spring Resort|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国民保養温泉地|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}}. An express bus service, nicknamed the "Romance Car," operated by {{ill|Chūtetsu Bus|ja|中鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūtetsu Bus|1=Chutetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11367772}} connects {{ill|Okayama Station|en|Okayama Station|WD=Q801293|lt=Okayama Station}}, {{ill|Tsuyama Station|en|Tsuyama Station|WD=Q844686|lt=Tsuyama Station}}, and Okutsu Onsen (stopping in front of the roadside station on National Route 179) with one round trip per day. == Access == * '''Automobile''': Approximately 25 minutes by car from the {{ill|Chūgoku Expressway|en|Chūgoku Expressway|WD=Q1091207|lt=Chūgoku Expressway}} and {{ill|Inshō Interchange|ja|院庄インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inshō Interchange|1=Innosho interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658129}}. * '''Railway''': Alight at {{ill|Tsuyama Station|en|Tsuyama Station|WD=Q844686|lt=Tsuyama Station}} on the {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR West|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西日本旅客鉄道|1=West Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q502125}} ({{ill|Kishin Line|en|Kishin Line|WD=Q869221|lt=Himeshin Line}}, {{ill|Tsuyama Line|en|Tsuyama Line|WD=Q7850362|lt=Tsuyama Line}}), then take the bus from bus terminal stop #5 operated by {{ill|Chūtetsu Hokubu Bus|ja|中鉄北部バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūtetsu Hokubu Bus|1=Chūtetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11367773}}: ** Board the Okutsu Onsen/Ishikoshi Line for about 50 minutes (operating 4 round trips per day; 2 round trips on weekends and holidays). ** Transfer to the {{ill|Gongo Bus|ja|ごんごバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gongo Bus|1=Gongo Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20044406}} West Loop line, and at either "Marunaka Inshō Store" or "Plant 5," transfer to a community bus bound for the Kamisaihara Promotion Center (operating 4 round trips per day). * '''Highway Bus''': From {{ill|Namba|ja|なんば|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namba|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ナンバ|1=Namba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1503790}} (via the {{ill|Osaka City Air Terminal|ja|なんば|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka City Air Terminal|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ナンバ|1=Namba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1503790}}) and from {{ill|Shin-Ōsaka Station|en|Shin-Ōsaka Station|WD=Q801438|lt=Shin-Osaka Station}} (via the Hankyu High-Speed Bus Shin-Osaka Terminal at the North Exit), take the {{ill|Nikkō Kōsokubasu|ja|日交高速バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō Kōsokubasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the line from {{ill|Kurayoshi, Tottori|en|Kurayoshi, Tottori|WD=Q642006|lt=Kurayoshi City}} to {{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|WD=Q48320|lt=Kobe City}}/Osaka. Alight at Okutsu Onsen-guchi (in front of the roadside station on {{ill|Japan National Route 179|en|Japan National Route 179|WD=Q6156867|lt=National Route 179}}); there is one round trip per day. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{WikiPortal|Hot Springs}} * {{ill|List_of_hot_springs_in_Japan|en|List_of_hot_springs_in_Japan|WD=Q11086467|lt=List of Hot Spring Resorts in Japan}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Okutsu Onsen}} * [https://www.mto.ne.jp/okutsuonsenkk/ Okutsu Onsen Tourism Association] * [http://kanko.town.kagamino.lg.jp/hot_spring/okutsu-spa/ Kagamino Town Tourism Navi: Okutsu Onsen] * [https://afw-at.com/page.php?id=146 Okutsu Onsen] – At Town Web Magazine ''' ''' '''''' {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|奥津温泉|version=104384327|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:奥津温泉]] [[zh:温泉]] [[ja:嬉野温泉]] [[ja:武雄温泉]] [[ja:日本書紀]] [[ja:南紀白浜温泉]] [[ja:行基]] [[ja:空海]] [[ja:一遍]] [[ja:仏教]] [[ja:坂上田村麻呂]] [[ja:高野聖]] [[ja:大友頼泰]] [[ja:浜脇温泉]] [[ja:別府温泉]] [[ja:鉄輪温泉]] [[ja:火男火売神社]] [[ja:下呂温泉]] [[ja:山中温泉]] [[ja:道後温泉]] [[ja:城崎温泉]] [[ja:湯田温泉]] [[ja:三朝温泉]] [[ja:山鹿温泉]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] [[ja:老神温泉]] [[zh:温泉神社]] [[zh:温泉寺]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] {{Commons category|Okutsu Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11445969}} [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Geography of Kagamino Town]] [[Category:Hot springs of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Legends]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Okutsu Onsen]][[ja:奥津温泉]] [[co:commons:Category:Okutsu Onsen]][[ja:奥津温泉]] [[sn:commons:Category:Okutsu Onsen]][[ja:奥津温泉]] {{infobox Onsen |Name = Okutsu Onsen |image = Okutsu Onsen 03.jpg |Comment = Day-trip Hot Spring Facility "Hanabijin no Sato" |Location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}} {{ill|Tomata District, Okayama|en|Tomata District, Okayama|WD=Q1131549|lt=Tomata District}} {{ill|Kagamino|en|Kagamino|WD=Q1155346|lt=Kagamino Town}} {{Location map many | Japan Okayama Prefecture | relief = 1 | caption = | width = 220 | float = center | lat_deg = 35.2229 | lon_deg = 133.9201 | pos = top | label = '''Okutsu Onsen''' | lat2_deg = 35.0547 | lon2_deg = 134.0030 | mark2 = blue pog.svg | pos2 = right | label2 = {{ill|Tsuyama Station|en|Tsuyama Station|WD=Q844686|lt=Tsuyama Station}} | lat3_deg = 35.0624 | lon3_deg = 133.9495 | mark3 = Turquoise pog.svg | pos3 = left | label3 = {{ill|Inshō Interchange|ja|院庄I.C.|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inshō Interchange|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | lat4_deg = 34.6663 | lon4_deg = 133.9185 | mark4 = blue pog.svg | pos4 = right | label4 = {{ill|Okayama Station|en|Okayama Station|WD=Q801293|lt=Okayama Station}} }}
Map of Okayama Prefecture
|Latitude Degrees = 35 |Latitude Minutes = 13 |Latitude Seconds = 22.7 | N/S = N |Longitude Degrees = 133 |Longitude Minutes = 55 |Longitude Seconds = 12.6 | E/W = E |Access = Railway: {{ill|Kishin Line|en|Kishin Line|WD=Q869221|lt=Himeshin Line}}, {{ill|Tsuyama Line|en|Tsuyama Line|WD=Q7850362|lt=Tsuyama Line}} – from {{ill|Tsuyama Station|en|Tsuyama Station|WD=Q844686|lt=Tsuyama Station}}, take {{ill|Chūtetsu Hokubu Bus|ja|中鉄北部バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūtetsu Hokubu Bus|1=Chūtetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11367773}} for about 50 minutes |Water Type = {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} |Temperature = 35 - 44 |Discharge = 1000 L (per minute) |pH = |Liquid Classification = {{ill|Alkaline|ja|アルカリ性|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alkaline|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 塩基|1=base|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11193}} |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Accommodations = |Total Capacity = |Annual Bath Visitors = |Statistical Year = |External links = [http://kanko.town.kagamino.lg.jp/hot_spring/okutsu-spa/ Kagamino Town Tourism Navi: Okutsu Onsen] |Special Notes = }} '''Okutsu Onsen''' (おくつ温泉) is a hot spring that emerges in {{ill|Kagamino|en|Kagamino|WD=Q1155346|lt=Kagamino Town}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}. Located in the area formerly known as {{ill|Mimasaka Province|en|Mimasaka Province|WD=Q936036|lt=Mimasaka Province}}, it is one of the "Mimasaka Three Hot Springs," together with {{ill|Yunogo_Onsen|en|Yunogo_Onsen|WD=Q8061351|lt=Yunogo Onsen}} and {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|ceb|Yubara hotspring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563486}}. == Water Quality == * {{ill|Alkaline Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alkaline Simple Hot Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} – Known as the "Hot Spring for Beauties"主婦の友社（編）『主婦の友ベストBooks 見直したい日本の「美」 日本の名湯100選』 p.134 主婦の友社 2010th year 3月10日発行 ISBN 978-4-07-270188-1. ** Source temperature: 35 - 44 ℃ ** Discharge: 1000 (L/min) == Onsen Town == [[File:Okutsu Onsen 02.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Footbath area]] A hot spring town is formed around the Okutsu Bridge spanning the {{ill|Yoshii River|en|Yoshii River|WD=Q11412583|lt=Yoshii River}}, with day-trip bathing facilities available. Along the riverbank, there are two {{ill|Outdoor Baths|ja|露天風呂|1=Outdoor hot spring baths|1=Outdoor hot spring baths|ko|노천탕|1=Outdoor hot spring baths|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outdoor Baths|13=User:Immanuelle/Outdoor hot spring baths|qq=}}; one of these is known as "Sentaku-yu" (Laundry Bath), where the practice of '''foot‑stepping laundry''' is famous. This method utilizes the drifting effect of the hot spring water—with a pH of 9.2—to aid in washing, a technique developed while keeping watch for bears and wolves in the surrounding mountains【湯の心旅】Okutsu Onsen (Okayama Prefecture) heralds spring with "foot‑stepping laundry" – washing away fatigue from within and without, as reported in the {{ill|The Nikkei|en|The Nikkei|WD=Q1077694|lt=Nikkei}} morning edition, December 21, 2020 (separate printing, NIKKEI Plus 1, page 13)主婦の友社（編）『主婦の友ベストBooks 見直したい日本の「美」 日本の名湯100選』 p.135 主婦の友社 2010th year 3月10日発行 ISBN 978-4-07-270188-1. Today, some locals still use the baths for laundry, and live demonstrations for tourists are held on Sundays and public holidays from March to November. Along National Route 179, there is a roadside station, {{ill|Michinoeki Okutsu Onsen|ja|道の駅奥津温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoeki Okutsu Onsen|1=Roadside station Okutsu-onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641244}}, though it does not include bathing facilities. Downstream, approximately 2 km from the laundry area along the Yoshii River, there are also {{ill|Han'nya-ji Onsen|ja|般若寺温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Han'nya-ji Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Ōdzuri Onsen|ja|大釣温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdzuri Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}; these are believed to be sourced from the same fault line as the upstream Okutsu Onsen, and in some contexts the entire area is collectively referred to as "Okutsu Onsen"日本温泉科学会（監修）『図説 日本の温泉 ―170温泉のサイエンス―』 p.133 朝倉書店 2020th year 3月1日発行 ISBN 978-4-254-16075-8. == History == According to the {{ill|Onsen Origin Myth|lt=Hot Spring Founding Legend|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|qq=}}, the hot spring was discovered by {{ill|Sukunabikona-no-mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo Period}}, it served as a site for hot spring treatment ({{ill|Tōji|ja|湯治|1=thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|1=Thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Thermal bath|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1004887}}) for the {{ill|Tsuyama Domain|en|Tsuyama Domain|WD=Q1058224|lt=Tsuyama Domain}}, and the area around the present-day foot‑stepping laundry is said to have once housed a "Locked Bath" ({{ill|Kagiyu|ja|鍵湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagiyu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), from which usage was restricted by domain lord {{ill|Mori Tadamasu|ja|森忠政|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Tadamasu|1=Mori Tadamasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11117105}}, who stationed guards to lock it. The cosmetics company {{ill|Kosé|en|Kosé|WD=Q18857532|lt=KOSÉ}} has developed a lotion based on the components of this hot spring. Foot‑stepping laundry has long been practiced; its process was even commemorated in a tanka by {{ill|Tekkan Yosano|en|Tekkan Yosano|WD=Q1206405|lt=Yosano Tekkan}}:
衣洗う　奥津のおとめ　河床の　清きに立ちて　踊るごと践む
However, a large fire in 1926 destroyed the hot spring town山村 順次 『47都道府県・温泉百科』 p.213 丸善出版 2015th year 12月30日発行 ISBN 978-4-621-08996-5. The area is also known as the setting of {{ill|Akitsu Springs|en|Akitsu Springs|WD=Q3212936|lt=Akitsu Onsen}}, a novel by {{ill|Fujiwara Shini|ja|藤原審爾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Shini|1=Shinji Fujiwara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623205}}, which was later adapted into a film in 1962 that boosted the prominence of Okutsu Onsen. On August 1, 1966, Okutsu Onsen was designated a {{ill|National Recreational Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Recreational Hot Spring Resort|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}}, and in 1991 it was designated as a {{ill|National Health Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保健温泉地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Health Hot Spring Resort|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国民保養温泉地|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}}. An express bus service, nicknamed the "Romance Car," operated by {{ill|Chūtetsu Bus|ja|中鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūtetsu Bus|1=Chutetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11367772}} connects {{ill|Okayama Station|en|Okayama Station|WD=Q801293|lt=Okayama Station}}, {{ill|Tsuyama Station|en|Tsuyama Station|WD=Q844686|lt=Tsuyama Station}}, and Okutsu Onsen (stopping in front of the roadside station on National Route 179) with one round trip per day. == Access == * '''Automobile''': Approximately 25 minutes by car from the {{ill|Chūgoku Expressway|en|Chūgoku Expressway|WD=Q1091207|lt=Chūgoku Expressway}} and {{ill|Inshō Interchange|ja|院庄インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inshō Interchange|1=Innosho interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658129}}. * '''Railway''': Alight at {{ill|Tsuyama Station|en|Tsuyama Station|WD=Q844686|lt=Tsuyama Station}} on the {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR West|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西日本旅客鉄道|1=West Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q502125}} ({{ill|Kishin Line|en|Kishin Line|WD=Q869221|lt=Himeshin Line}}, {{ill|Tsuyama Line|en|Tsuyama Line|WD=Q7850362|lt=Tsuyama Line}}), then take the bus from bus terminal stop #5 operated by {{ill|Chūtetsu Hokubu Bus|ja|中鉄北部バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūtetsu Hokubu Bus|1=Chūtetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11367773}}: ** Board the Okutsu Onsen/Ishikoshi Line for about 50 minutes (operating 4 round trips per day; 2 round trips on weekends and holidays). ** Transfer to the {{ill|Gongo Bus|ja|ごんごバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gongo Bus|1=Gongo Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20044406}} West Loop line, and at either "Marunaka Inshō Store" or "Plant 5," transfer to a community bus bound for the Kamisaihara Promotion Center (operating 4 round trips per day). * '''Highway Bus''': From {{ill|Namba|ja|なんば|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namba|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ナンバ|1=Namba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1503790}} (via the {{ill|Osaka City Air Terminal|ja|なんば|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka City Air Terminal|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ナンバ|1=Namba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1503790}}) and from {{ill|Shin-Ōsaka Station|en|Shin-Ōsaka Station|WD=Q801438|lt=Shin-Osaka Station}} (via the Hankyu High-Speed Bus Shin-Osaka Terminal at the North Exit), take the {{ill|Nikkō Kōsokubasu|ja|日交高速バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō Kōsokubasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the line from {{ill|Kurayoshi, Tottori|en|Kurayoshi, Tottori|WD=Q642006|lt=Kurayoshi City}} to {{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|WD=Q48320|lt=Kobe City}}/Osaka. Alight at Okutsu Onsen-guchi (in front of the roadside station on {{ill|Japan National Route 179|en|Japan National Route 179|WD=Q6156867|lt=National Route 179}}); there is one round trip per day. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{WikiPortal|Hot Springs}} * {{ill|List_of_hot_springs_in_Japan|en|List_of_hot_springs_in_Japan|WD=Q11086467|lt=List of Hot Spring Resorts in Japan}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Okutsu Onsen}} * [https://www.mto.ne.jp/okutsuonsenkk/ Okutsu Onsen Tourism Association] * [http://kanko.town.kagamino.lg.jp/hot_spring/okutsu-spa/ Kagamino Town Tourism Navi: Okutsu Onsen] * [https://afw-at.com/page.php?id=146 Okutsu Onsen] – At Town Web Magazine ''' ''' '''''' {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|奥津温泉|version=104384327|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:奥津温泉]] [[zh:温泉]] [[ja:嬉野温泉]] [[ja:武雄温泉]] [[ja:日本書紀]] [[ja:南紀白浜温泉]] [[ja:行基]] [[ja:空海]] [[ja:一遍]] [[ja:仏教]] [[ja:坂上田村麻呂]] [[ja:高野聖]] [[ja:大友頼泰]] [[ja:浜脇温泉]] [[ja:別府温泉]] [[ja:鉄輪温泉]] [[ja:火男火売神社]] [[ja:下呂温泉]] [[ja:山中温泉]] [[ja:道後温泉]] [[ja:城崎温泉]] [[ja:湯田温泉]] [[ja:三朝温泉]] [[ja:山鹿温泉]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] [[ja:老神温泉]] [[zh:温泉神社]] [[zh:温泉寺]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] {{Commons category|Okutsu Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11445969}} [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Geography of Kagamino Town]] [[Category:Hot springs of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Legends]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

revision.comment: Fixing this nonstandard dash thing that makes all of the redirects break
revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Okutsu Onsen]][[ja:奥津温泉]] [[co:commons:Category:Okutsu Onsen]][[ja:奥津温泉]] [[sn:commons:Category:Okutsu Onsen]][[ja:奥津温泉]] {{infobox Onsen |Name = Okutsu Onsen |image = Okutsu Onsen 03.jpg |Comment = Day-trip Hot Spring Facility "Hanabijin no Sato" |Location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}} {{ill|Tomata District, Okayama|en|Tomata District, Okayama|WD=Q1131549|lt=Tomata District}} {{ill|Kagamino|en|Kagamino|WD=Q1155346|lt=Kagamino Town}} {{Location map many | Japan Okayama Prefecture | relief = 1 | caption = | width = 220 | float = center | lat_deg = 35.2229 | lon_deg = 133.9201 | pos = top | label = '''Okutsu Onsen''' | lat2_deg = 35.0547 | lon2_deg = 134.0030 | mark2 = blue pog.svg | pos2 = right | label2 = {{ill|Tsuyama Station|en|Tsuyama Station|WD=Q844686|lt=Tsuyama Station}} | lat3_deg = 35.0624 | lon3_deg = 133.9495 | mark3 = Turquoise pog.svg | pos3 = left | label3 = {{ill|Inshō Interchange|ja|院庄I.C.|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inshō Interchange|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | lat4_deg = 34.6663 | lon4_deg = 133.9185 | mark4 = blue pog.svg | pos4 = right | label4 = {{ill|Okayama Station|en|Okayama Station|WD=Q801293|lt=Okayama Station}} }}
Map of Okayama Prefecture
|Latitude Degrees = 35 |Latitude Minutes = 13 |Latitude Seconds = 22.7 | N/S = N |Longitude Degrees = 133 |Longitude Minutes = 55 |Longitude Seconds = 12.6 | E/W = E |Access = Railway: {{ill|Kishin Line|en|Kishin Line|WD=Q869221|lt=Himeshin Line}}, {{ill|Tsuyama Line|en|Tsuyama Line|WD=Q7850362|lt=Tsuyama Line}} – from {{ill|Tsuyama Station|en|Tsuyama Station|WD=Q844686|lt=Tsuyama Station}}, take {{ill|Chūtetsu Hokubu Bus|ja|中鉄北部バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūtetsu Hokubu Bus|1=Chūtetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11367773}} for about 50 minutes |Water Type = {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} |Temperature = 35 - 44 |Discharge = 1000 L (per minute) |pH = |Liquid Classification = {{ill|Alkaline|ja|アルカリ性|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alkaline|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 塩基|1=base|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11193}} |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Accommodations = |Total Capacity = |Annual Bath Visitors = |Statistical Year = |External links = [http://kanko.town.kagamino.lg.jp/hot_spring/okutsu-spa/ Kagamino Town Tourism Navi: Okutsu Onsen] |Special Notes = }} '''Okutsu Onsen''' (おくつ温泉) is a hot spring that emerges in {{ill|Kagamino|en|Kagamino|WD=Q1155346|lt=Kagamino Town}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}. Located in the area formerly known as {{ill|Mimasaka Province|en|Mimasaka Province|WD=Q936036|lt=Mimasaka Province}}, it is one of the "Mimasaka Three Hot Springs," together with {{ill|Yunogo_Onsen|en|Yunogo_Onsen|WD=Q8061351|lt=Yunogo Onsen}} and {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|ceb|Yubara hotspring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563486}}. == Water Quality == * {{ill|Alkaline Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alkaline Simple Hot Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} – Known as the "Hot Spring for Beauties"主婦の友社（編）『主婦の友ベストBooks 見直したい日本の「美」 日本の名湯100選』 p.134 主婦の友社 2010th year 3月10日発行 ISBN 978-4-07-270188-1. ** Source temperature: 35 - 44 ℃ ** Discharge: 1000 (L/min) == Onsen Town == [[File:Okutsu Onsen 02.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Footbath area]] A hot spring town is formed around the Okutsu Bridge spanning the {{ill|Yoshii River|en|Yoshii River|WD=Q11412583|lt=Yoshii River}}, with day-trip bathing facilities available. Along the riverbank, there are two {{ill|Outdoor Baths|ja|露天風呂|1=Outdoor hot spring baths|1=Outdoor hot spring baths|ko|노천탕|1=Outdoor hot spring baths|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outdoor Baths|13=User:Immanuelle/Outdoor hot spring baths|qq=}}; one of these is known as "Sentaku-yu" (Laundry Bath), where the practice of '''foot-stepping laundry''' is famous. This method utilizes the drifting effect of the hot spring water—with a pH of 9.2—to aid in washing, a technique developed while keeping watch for bears and wolves in the surrounding mountains【湯の心旅】Okutsu Onsen (Okayama Prefecture) heralds spring with "foot-stepping laundry" – washing away fatigue from within and without, as reported in the {{ill|The Nikkei|en|The Nikkei|WD=Q1077694|lt=Nikkei}} morning edition, December 21, 2020 (separate printing, NIKKEI Plus 1, page 13)主婦の友社（編）『主婦の友ベストBooks 見直したい日本の「美」 日本の名湯100選』 p.135 主婦の友社 2010th year 3月10日発行 ISBN 978-4-07-270188-1. Today, some locals still use the baths for laundry, and live demonstrations for tourists are held on Sundays and public holidays from March to November. Along National Route 179, there is a roadside station, {{ill|Michinoeki Okutsu Onsen|ja|道の駅奥津温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoeki Okutsu Onsen|1=Roadside station Okutsu-onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641244}}, though it does not include bathing facilities. Downstream, approximately 2 km from the laundry area along the Yoshii River, there are also {{ill|Han'nya-ji Onsen|ja|般若寺温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Han'nya-ji Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Ōdzuri Onsen|ja|大釣温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdzuri Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}; these are believed to be sourced from the same fault line as the upstream Okutsu Onsen, and in some contexts the entire area is collectively referred to as "Okutsu Onsen"日本温泉科学会（監修）『図説 日本の温泉 ―170温泉のサイエンス―』 p.133 朝倉書店 2020th year 3月1日発行 ISBN 978-4-254-16075-8. == History == According to the {{ill|Onsen Origin Myth|lt=Hot Spring Founding Legend|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|qq=}}, the hot spring was discovered by {{ill|Sukunabikona-no-mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo Period}}, it served as a site for hot spring treatment ({{ill|Tōji|ja|湯治|1=thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|1=Thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Thermal bath|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1004887}}) for the {{ill|Tsuyama Domain|en|Tsuyama Domain|WD=Q1058224|lt=Tsuyama Domain}}, and the area around the present-day foot-stepping laundry is said to have once housed a "Locked Bath" ({{ill|Kagiyu|ja|鍵湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagiyu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), from which usage was restricted by domain lord {{ill|Mori Tadamasu|ja|森忠政|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Tadamasu|1=Mori Tadamasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11117105}}, who stationed guards to lock it. The cosmetics company {{ill|Kosé|en|Kosé|WD=Q18857532|lt=KOSÉ}} has developed a lotion based on the components of this hot spring. Foot-stepping laundry has long been practiced; its process was even commemorated in a tanka by {{ill|Tekkan Yosano|en|Tekkan Yosano|WD=Q1206405|lt=Yosano Tekkan}}:
衣洗う　奥津のおとめ　河床の　清きに立ちて　踊るごと践む
However, a large fire in 1926 destroyed the hot spring town山村 順次 『47都道府県・温泉百科』 p.213 丸善出版 2015th year 12月30日発行 ISBN 978-4-621-08996-5. The area is also known as the setting of {{ill|Akitsu Springs|en|Akitsu Springs|WD=Q3212936|lt=Akitsu Onsen}}, a novel by {{ill|Fujiwara Shini|ja|藤原審爾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Shini|1=Shinji Fujiwara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623205}}, which was later adapted into a film in 1962 that boosted the prominence of Okutsu Onsen. On August 1, 1966, Okutsu Onsen was designated a {{ill|National Recreational Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Recreational Hot Spring Resort|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}}, and in 1991 it was designated as a {{ill|National Health Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保健温泉地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Health Hot Spring Resort|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国民保養温泉地|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}}. An express bus service, nicknamed the "Romance Car," operated by {{ill|Chūtetsu Bus|ja|中鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūtetsu Bus|1=Chutetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11367772}} connects {{ill|Okayama Station|en|Okayama Station|WD=Q801293|lt=Okayama Station}}, {{ill|Tsuyama Station|en|Tsuyama Station|WD=Q844686|lt=Tsuyama Station}}, and Okutsu Onsen (stopping in front of the roadside station on National Route 179) with one round trip per day. == Access == * '''Automobile''': Approximately 25 minutes by car from the {{ill|Chūgoku Expressway|en|Chūgoku Expressway|WD=Q1091207|lt=Chūgoku Expressway}} and {{ill|Inshō Interchange|ja|院庄インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inshō Interchange|1=Innosho interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658129}}. * '''Railway''': Alight at {{ill|Tsuyama Station|en|Tsuyama Station|WD=Q844686|lt=Tsuyama Station}} on the {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR West|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西日本旅客鉄道|1=West Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q502125}} ({{ill|Kishin Line|en|Kishin Line|WD=Q869221|lt=Himeshin Line}}, {{ill|Tsuyama Line|en|Tsuyama Line|WD=Q7850362|lt=Tsuyama Line}}), then take the bus from bus terminal stop #5 operated by {{ill|Chūtetsu Hokubu Bus|ja|中鉄北部バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūtetsu Hokubu Bus|1=Chūtetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11367773}}: ** Board the Okutsu Onsen/Ishikoshi Line for about 50 minutes (operating 4 round trips per day; 2 round trips on weekends and holidays). ** Transfer to the {{ill|Gongo Bus|ja|ごんごバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gongo Bus|1=Gongo Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20044406}} West Loop line, and at either "Marunaka Inshō Store" or "Plant 5," transfer to a community bus bound for the Kamisaihara Promotion Center (operating 4 round trips per day). * '''Highway Bus''': From {{ill|Namba|ja|なんば|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namba|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ナンバ|1=Namba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1503790}} (via the {{ill|Osaka City Air Terminal|ja|なんば|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka City Air Terminal|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ナンバ|1=Namba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1503790}}) and from {{ill|Shin-Ōsaka Station|en|Shin-Ōsaka Station|WD=Q801438|lt=Shin-Osaka Station}} (via the Hankyu High-Speed Bus Shin-Osaka Terminal at the North Exit), take the {{ill|Nikkō Kōsokubasu|ja|日交高速バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō Kōsokubasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the line from {{ill|Kurayoshi, Tottori|en|Kurayoshi, Tottori|WD=Q642006|lt=Kurayoshi City}} to {{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|WD=Q48320|lt=Kobe City}}/Osaka. Alight at Okutsu Onsen-guchi (in front of the roadside station on {{ill|Japan National Route 179|en|Japan National Route 179|WD=Q6156867|lt=National Route 179}}); there is one round trip per day. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{WikiPortal|Hot Springs}} * {{ill|List_of_hot_springs_in_Japan|en|List_of_hot_springs_in_Japan|WD=Q11086467|lt=List of Hot Spring Resorts in Japan}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Okutsu Onsen}} * [https://www.mto.ne.jp/okutsuonsenkk/ Okutsu Onsen Tourism Association] * [http://kanko.town.kagamino.lg.jp/hot_spring/okutsu-spa/ Kagamino Town Tourism Navi: Okutsu Onsen] * [https://afw-at.com/page.php?id=146 Okutsu Onsen] – At Town Web Magazine ''' ''' '''''' {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|奥津温泉|version=104384327|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:奥津温泉]] [[zh:温泉]] [[ja:嬉野温泉]] [[ja:武雄温泉]] [[ja:日本書紀]] [[ja:南紀白浜温泉]] [[ja:行基]] [[ja:空海]] [[ja:一遍]] [[ja:仏教]] [[ja:坂上田村麻呂]] [[ja:高野聖]] [[ja:大友頼泰]] [[ja:浜脇温泉]] [[ja:別府温泉]] [[ja:鉄輪温泉]] [[ja:火男火売神社]] [[ja:下呂温泉]] [[ja:山中温泉]] [[ja:道後温泉]] [[ja:城崎温泉]] [[ja:湯田温泉]] [[ja:三朝温泉]] [[ja:山鹿温泉]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] [[ja:老神温泉]] [[zh:温泉神社]] [[zh:温泉寺]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] {{Commons category|Okutsu Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11445969}} [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Geography of Kagamino Town]] [[Category:Hot springs of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Legends]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Okutsu Onsen]][[ja:奥津温泉]] [[co:commons:Category:Okutsu Onsen]][[ja:奥津温泉]] [[sn:commons:Category:Okutsu Onsen]][[ja:奥津温泉]] {{infobox Onsen |Name = Okutsu Onsen |image = Okutsu Onsen 03.jpg |Comment = Day-trip Hot Spring Facility "Hanabijin no Sato" |Location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}} {{ill|Tomata District, Okayama|en|Tomata District, Okayama|WD=Q1131549|lt=Tomata District|1=Tomata District, Okayama}} {{ill|Kagamino|en|Kagamino|WD=Q1155346|lt=Kagamino Town|1=Kagamino}} {{Location map many | Japan Okayama Prefecture | relief = 1 | caption = | width = 220 | float = center | lat_deg = 35.2229 | lon_deg = 133.9201 | pos = top | label = '''Okutsu Onsen''' | lat2_deg = 35.0547 | lon2_deg = 134.0030 | mark2 = blue pog.svg | pos2 = right | label2 = {{ill|Tsuyama Station|en|Tsuyama Station|WD=Q844686|lt=Tsuyama Station|1=Tsuyama Station}} | lat3_deg = 35.0624 | lon3_deg = 133.9495 | mark3 = Turquoise pog.svg | pos3 = left | label3 = {{ill|Inshō Interchange|ja|院庄I.C.|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inshō Interchange|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | lat4_deg = 34.6663 | lon4_deg = 133.9185 | mark4 = blue pog.svg | pos4 = right | label4 = {{ill|Okayama Station|en|Okayama Station|WD=Q801293|lt=Okayama Station|1=Okayama Station}} }}
Map of Okayama Prefecture
|Latitude Degrees = 35 |Latitude Minutes = 13 |Latitude Seconds = 22.7 | N/S = N |Longitude Degrees = 133 |Longitude Minutes = 55 |Longitude Seconds = 12.6 | E/W = E |Access = Railway: {{ill|Kishin Line|en|Kishin Line|WD=Q869221|lt=Himeshin Line|1=Kishin Line}}, {{ill|Tsuyama Line|en|Tsuyama Line|WD=Q7850362|lt=Tsuyama Line|1=Tsuyama Line}} – from {{ill|Tsuyama Station|en|Tsuyama Station|WD=Q844686|lt=Tsuyama Station|1=Tsuyama Station}}, take {{ill|Chūtetsu Hokubu Bus|ja|中鉄北部バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūtetsu Hokubu Bus|1=Chūtetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11367773}} for about 50 minutes |Water Type = {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} |Temperature = 35 - 44 |Discharge = 1000 L (per minute) |pH = |Liquid Classification = {{ill|Alkaline|ja|アルカリ性|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alkaline|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 塩基|1=Base (chemistry)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11193}} |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Accommodations = |Total Capacity = |Annual Bath Visitors = |Statistical Year = |External links = [http://kanko.town.kagamino.lg.jp/hot_spring/okutsu-spa/ Kagamino Town Tourism Navi: Okutsu Onsen] |Special Notes = }} '''Okutsu Onsen''' (おくつ温泉) is a hot spring that emerges in {{ill|Kagamino|en|Kagamino|WD=Q1155346|lt=Kagamino Town|1=Kagamino}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}}. Located in the area formerly known as {{ill|Mimasaka Province|en|Mimasaka Province|WD=Q936036|lt=Mimasaka Province|1=Mimasaka Province}}, it is one of the "Mimasaka Three Hot Springs," together with {{ill|Yunogo_Onsen|en|Yunogo_Onsen|WD=Q8061351|lt=Yunogo Onsen|1=Yunogo Onsen}} and {{ill|Yubara Onsen|ja|湯原温泉|1=Yubara Onsen|2=ja|3=湯原温泉|4=ceb|5=Yubara hotspring|ceb|Yubara hotspring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563486}}. == Water Quality == * {{ill|Alkaline Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alkaline Simple Hot Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} – Known as the "Hot Spring for Beauties"主婦の友社（編）『主婦の友ベストBooks 見直したい日本の「美」 日本の名湯100選』 p.134 主婦の友社 2010th year 3月10日発行 ISBN 978-4-07-270188-1. ** Source temperature: 35 - 44 ℃ ** Discharge: 1000 (L/min) == Onsen Town == [[File:Okutsu Onsen 02.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Footbath area]] A hot spring town is formed around the Okutsu Bridge spanning the {{ill|Yoshii River|en|Yoshii River|WD=Q11412583|lt=Yoshii River|1=Yoshii River}}, with day-trip bathing facilities available. Along the riverbank, there are two {{ill|Outdoor Baths|ja|露天風呂|1=Outdoor hot spring baths|1=Outdoor hot spring baths|ko|노천탕|1=Outdoor hot spring baths|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outdoor Baths|13=User:Immanuelle/Outdoor hot spring baths|qq=}}; one of these is known as "Sentaku-yu" (Laundry Bath), where the practice of '''foot-stepping laundry''' is famous. This method utilizes the drifting effect of the hot spring water—with a pH of 9.2—to aid in washing, a technique developed while keeping watch for bears and wolves in the surrounding mountains【湯の心旅】Okutsu Onsen (Okayama Prefecture) heralds spring with "foot-stepping laundry" – washing away fatigue from within and without, as reported in the {{ill|The Nikkei|en|The Nikkei|WD=Q1077694|lt=Nikkei|1=The Nikkei}} morning edition, December 21, 2020 (separate printing, NIKKEI Plus 1, page 13)主婦の友社（編）『主婦の友ベストBooks 見直したい日本の「美」 日本の名湯100選』 p.135 主婦の友社 2010th year 3月10日発行 ISBN 978-4-07-270188-1. Today, some locals still use the baths for laundry, and live demonstrations for tourists are held on Sundays and public holidays from March to November. Along National Route 179, there is a roadside station, {{ill|Michinoeki Okutsu Onsen|ja|道の駅奥津温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoeki Okutsu Onsen|1=Roadside station Okutsu-onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641244}}, though it does not include bathing facilities. Downstream, approximately 2 km from the laundry area along the Yoshii River, there are also {{ill|Han'nya-ji Onsen|ja|般若寺温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Han'nya-ji Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Ōdzuri Onsen|ja|大釣温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdzuri Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}; these are believed to be sourced from the same fault line as the upstream Okutsu Onsen, and in some contexts the entire area is collectively referred to as "Okutsu Onsen"日本温泉科学会（監修）『図説 日本の温泉 ―170温泉のサイエンス―』 p.133 朝倉書店 2020th year 3月1日発行 ISBN 978-4-254-16075-8. == History == According to the {{ill|Onsen Origin Myth|lt=Hot Spring Founding Legend|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|qq=}}, the hot spring was discovered by {{ill|Sukunabikona-no-mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo Period|1=Edo period}}, it served as a site for hot spring treatment ({{ill|Tōji|ja|湯治|1=Thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|1=Thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Thermal bath|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1004887}}) for the {{ill|Tsuyama Domain|en|Tsuyama Domain|WD=Q1058224|lt=Tsuyama Domain|1=Tsuyama Domain}}, and the area around the present-day foot-stepping laundry is said to have once housed a "Locked Bath" ({{ill|Kagiyu|ja|鍵湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagiyu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), from which usage was restricted by domain lord {{ill|Mori Tadamasu|ja|森忠政|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Tadamasu|1=Mori Tadamasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11117105}}, who stationed guards to lock it. The cosmetics company {{ill|Kosé|en|Kosé|WD=Q18857532|lt=KOSÉ|1=Kosé}} has developed a lotion based on the components of this hot spring. Foot-stepping laundry has long been practiced; its process was even commemorated in a tanka by {{ill|Tekkan Yosano|en|Tekkan Yosano|WD=Q1206405|lt=Yosano Tekkan|1=Tekkan Yosano}}:
衣洗う　奥津のおとめ　河床の　清きに立ちて　踊るごと践む
However, a large fire in 1926 destroyed the hot spring town山村 順次 『47都道府県・温泉百科』 p.213 丸善出版 2015th year 12月30日発行 ISBN 978-4-621-08996-5. The area is also known as the setting of {{ill|Akitsu Springs|en|Akitsu Springs|WD=Q3212936|lt=Akitsu Onsen|1=Akitsu Springs}}, a novel by {{ill|Fujiwara Shini|ja|藤原審爾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Shini|1=Shinji Fujiwara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623205}}, which was later adapted into a film in 1962 that boosted the prominence of Okutsu Onsen. On August 1, 1966, Okutsu Onsen was designated a {{ill|National Recreational Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Recreational Hot Spring Resort|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}}, and in 1991 it was designated as a {{ill|National Health Hot Spring Resort|ja|国民保健温泉地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Health Hot Spring Resort|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国民保養温泉地|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}}. An express bus service, nicknamed the "Romance Car," operated by {{ill|Chūtetsu Bus|ja|中鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūtetsu Bus|1=Chutetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11367772}} connects {{ill|Okayama Station|en|Okayama Station|WD=Q801293|lt=Okayama Station|1=Okayama Station}}, {{ill|Tsuyama Station|en|Tsuyama Station|WD=Q844686|lt=Tsuyama Station|1=Tsuyama Station}}, and Okutsu Onsen (stopping in front of the roadside station on National Route 179) with one round trip per day. == Access == * '''Automobile''': Approximately 25 minutes by car from the {{ill|Chūgoku Expressway|en|Chūgoku Expressway|WD=Q1091207|lt=Chūgoku Expressway|1=Chūgoku Expressway}} and {{ill|Inshō Interchange|ja|院庄インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inshō Interchange|1=Innosho interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658129}}. * '''Railway''': Alight at {{ill|Tsuyama Station|en|Tsuyama Station|WD=Q844686|lt=Tsuyama Station|1=Tsuyama Station}} on the {{ill|JR West|ja|JR西日本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR West|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西日本旅客鉄道|1=West Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q502125}} ({{ill|Kishin Line|en|Kishin Line|WD=Q869221|lt=Himeshin Line|1=Kishin Line}}, {{ill|Tsuyama Line|en|Tsuyama Line|WD=Q7850362|lt=Tsuyama Line|1=Tsuyama Line}}), then take the bus from bus terminal stop #5 operated by {{ill|Chūtetsu Hokubu Bus|ja|中鉄北部バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūtetsu Hokubu Bus|1=Chūtetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11367773}}: ** Board the Okutsu Onsen/Ishikoshi Line for about 50 minutes (operating 4 round trips per day; 2 round trips on weekends and holidays). ** Transfer to the {{ill|Gongo Bus|ja|ごんごバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gongo Bus|1=Q20044406|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20044406}} West Loop line, and at either "Marunaka Inshō Store" or "Plant 5," transfer to a community bus bound for the Kamisaihara Promotion Center (operating 4 round trips per day). * '''Highway Bus''': From {{ill|Namba|ja|なんば|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namba|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ナンバ|1=Namba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1503790}} (via the {{ill|Osaka City Air Terminal|ja|なんば|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka City Air Terminal|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ナンバ|1=Namba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1503790}}) and from {{ill|Shin-Ōsaka Station|en|Shin-Ōsaka Station|WD=Q801438|lt=Shin-Osaka Station|1=Shin-Ōsaka Station}} (via the Hankyu High-Speed Bus Shin-Osaka Terminal at the North Exit), take the {{ill|Nikkō Kōsokubasu|ja|日交高速バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō Kōsokubasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the line from {{ill|Kurayoshi, Tottori|en|Kurayoshi, Tottori|WD=Q642006|lt=Kurayoshi City|1=Kurayoshi, Tottori}} to {{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|WD=Q48320|lt=Kobe City|1=Kobe}}/Osaka. Alight at Okutsu Onsen-guchi (in front of the roadside station on {{ill|Japan National Route 179|en|Japan National Route 179|WD=Q6156867|lt=National Route 179|1=Japan National Route 179}}); there is one round trip per day. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{WikiPortal|Hot Springs}} * {{ill|List_of_hot_springs_in_Japan|en|List_of_hot_springs_in_Japan|WD=Q11086467|lt=List of Hot Spring Resorts in Japan|1=List of hot springs in Japan}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Okutsu Onsen}} * [https://www.mto.ne.jp/okutsuonsenkk/ Okutsu Onsen Tourism Association] * [http://kanko.town.kagamino.lg.jp/hot_spring/okutsu-spa/ Kagamino Town Tourism Navi: Okutsu Onsen] * [https://afw-at.com/page.php?id=146 Okutsu Onsen] – At Town Web Magazine ''' ''' '''''' {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|奥津温泉|version=104384327|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:奥津温泉]] [[zh:温泉]] [[ja:嬉野温泉]] [[ja:武雄温泉]] [[ja:日本書紀]] [[ja:南紀白浜温泉]] [[ja:行基]] [[ja:空海]] [[ja:一遍]] [[ja:仏教]] [[ja:坂上田村麻呂]] [[ja:高野聖]] [[ja:大友頼泰]] [[ja:浜脇温泉]] [[ja:別府温泉]] [[ja:鉄輪温泉]] [[ja:火男火売神社]] [[ja:下呂温泉]] [[ja:山中温泉]] [[ja:道後温泉]] [[ja:城崎温泉]] [[ja:湯田温泉]] [[ja:三朝温泉]] [[ja:山鹿温泉]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] [[ja:老神温泉]] [[zh:温泉神社]] [[zh:温泉寺]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] {{Commons category|Okutsu Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11445969}} [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Geography of Kagamino Town]] [[Category:Hot springs of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Legends]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

Along the riverbank, there are two {{ill|Outdoor Baths|ja|露天風呂|1=Outdoor hot spring baths|1=Outdoor hot spring baths|ko|노천탕|1=Outdoor hot spring baths|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outdoor Baths|13=User:Immanuelle/Outdoor hot spring baths|qq=}}; one of these is known as "Sentaku-yu" (Laundry Bath), where the practice of '''foot-stepping laundry''' is famous. This method utilizes the drifting effect of the hot spring water—with a pH of 9.2—to aid in washing, a technique developed while keeping watch for bears and wolves in the surrounding mountains【湯の心旅】Okutsu Onsen (Okayama Prefecture) heralds spring with "foot-stepping laundry" – washing away fatigue from within and without, as reported in the {{ill|The Nikkei|en|The Nikkei|WD=Q1077694|lt=Nikkei|1=The Nikkei}} morning edition, December 21, 2020 (separate printing, NIKKEI Plus 1, page 13)主婦の友社（編）『主婦の友ベストBooks 見直したい日本の「美」 日本の名湯100選』 p.135 主婦の友社 2010th year 3月10日発行 ISBN 978-4-07-270188-1.
